items.article_text:
On Rendering the Sky, Sunsets, and Planets
There’s this photo that’s been sitting on my inspiration board for a while, of the space shuttle Endeavour, suspended in space in low Earth orbit at sunset. It shows Earth’s upper atmosphere as a backdrop, featuring beautiful, colorful layers ranging from dark orange to blue before fading away into the deep black of space. Not only is that gradient of color aesthetically pleasing, but the phenomenon behind those colors, atmospheric scattering, is even more of an interesting topic once you start looking into how it works and how to reproduce it.
I wanted to build my own version of this effect with shaders, rendering the sky’s distinctive blue color and realistic sunsets and sunrises directly in the browser. The goal was to get as close as I could to that photo, while also moving toward the kind of atmospheric rendering often seen in games and other shader-based media.
Here’s a compilation of what came out of this month-long journey, all running in real time:
I didn’t originally plan on writing about this subject, but the enthusiasm around the recent Artemis II mission, combined with my own interest in all things space, made it feel worth exploring in depth. It also felt like the perfect opportunity to build an interactive experience that could make the topic more accessible. In this write-up, we’ll see how to implement an atmospheric scattering shader post-processing effect step-by-step, starting with the implementation of the different building blocks (raymarching, Rayleigh and Mie scattering, as well as ozone absorption) to render a realistic sky dome, and then adapt the result to render it as an atmospheric shell around a planet. Finally, we'll look into Sebastian Hillaire’s LUT-based approach for a more performant result, or at least my attempt at implementing it, as this was very much the stepping outside of my comfort zone phase for this project.
You may have, at some point or another, tried to slap a blue gradient background behind some of your work in an attempt to give it a more "atmospheric" look and call it a day, but quickly noticed doing so never feels quite right [1](https://blog.maximeheckel.com#fn-1).
For a more true to life implementation, we must treat the sky and its color as the result of light interacting with air and its constituents, while taking into account several variables, such the altitude of the observer, the amount of dust, the time of day, etc, all of that in a volume.
With that established, our goal for this first part is to use this as guiding principle to lay the foundation for our atmosphere shader, and get to a result that feels almost indistinguishable from a real sky, at any time of the day.
Sampling Atmospheric Density
Much like how we’d approach [volumetric clouds](https://blog.maximeheckel.com/posts/real-time-cloudscapes-with-volumetric-raymarching/) or [volumetric light](https://blog.maximeheckel.com/posts/shaping-light-volumetric-lighting-with-post-processing-and-raymarching/), one easy way to sample the atmosphere is through raymarching. We can cast rays from the camera’s position into the scene and step through the transparent medium to answer the two following questions:
- How much light survives traveling through the atmosphere? This is the transmittance term.
- How much light is redirected toward the camera at each sample? Also known as scattering.
To answer the first one, we need to accumulate the atmospheric density encountered along the ray to obtain what is known as the optical depth. We will model this using the Rayleigh density function, which tells us how much "air" there is at a given altitude h
. This is important to take into account that the atmosphere gets thinner as altitude increases.
Sampling Rayleigh density and accumulating optical depth
1const float RAYLEIGH_SCALE_HEIGHT = 8.0; // km2const float ATMOSPHERE_HEIGHT = 100.0; // km - Karman line3const float VIEW_DISTANCE = 200.0; // km4const int PRIMARY_STEPS = 24;5const ve
items.article_text_zh:
关于渲染天空、日落与行星

我的灵感板上一直存着这样一张照片：航天飞机"奋进号"在日落时分悬浮于近地轨道太空中。背景是地球高层大气，呈现出从深橙色到蓝色再到深邃太空黑色的美丽彩色分层。这种色彩渐变不仅赏心悦目，其背后的物理现象——大气散射——更是一个值得深入探讨的有趣话题，无论是其原理还是如何复现。

我想用着色器在浏览器中直接构建自己的版本，渲染出天空独特的蓝色以及逼真的日出日落效果。目标是尽可能接近那张照片，同时向游戏和其他基于着色器的媒体中常见的大气渲染效果靠拢。

以下是我这一个月探索的成果汇编，全部实时运行：

我原本没打算写这个主题，但最近阿尔忒弥斯二号任务引发的热情，加上我个人对太空事物的浓厚兴趣，让我觉得值得深入探讨。这也恰好是构建一个交互式体验、让这个话题更易理解的绝佳机会。在这篇文章中，我们将逐步实现一个大气散射着色器的后处理效果，从构建不同的基础模块（光线步进、瑞利散射、米氏散射以及臭氧吸收）开始，渲染出逼真的天空穹顶，然后将结果调整为围绕行星的大气层外壳。最后，我们将探讨塞巴斯蒂安·希莱尔基于查找表（LUT）的高性能方法——至少是我尝试实现它的过程，因为这正是本项目让我走出舒适区的阶段。

你可能曾在某个时候尝试过在你的作品后面放一个蓝色渐变背景，想营造一种"大气"的感觉，然后就此收工，但很快发现这样做总是不太对劲[1](https://blog.maximeheckel.com#fn-1)。

为了实现更逼真的效果，我们必须将天空及其颜色视为光与空气及其成分相互作用的结果，同时考虑多个变量，如观察者海拔高度、尘埃量、一天中的时间等，所有这些都在一个体积空间内进行。

明确了这一点，我们第一部分的目标就是以这个为指导原则，为我们的大气着色器奠定基础，最终得到在任何时间都几乎与真实天空难以区分的效果。

**采样大气密度**

就像我们处理[体积云](https://blog.maximeheckel.com/posts/real-time-cloudscapes-with-volumetric-raymarching/)或[体积光](https://blog.maximeheckel.com/posts/shaping-light-volumetric-lighting-with-post-processing-and-raymarching/)一样，采样大气的一种简单方法是通过光线步进。我们可以从相机位置向场景中发射光线，步进穿过透明介质，回答以下两个问题：

- 有多少光能在穿过大气后幸存？这是透射率项。
- 在每个采样点，有多少光被重新定向到相机？这也称为散射。

要回答第一个问题，我们需要累积沿光线遇到的大气密度，以获得所谓的光学深度。我们将使用瑞利密度函数来建模，该函数告诉我们给定海拔高度 \( h \) 处有多少"空气"。这对于考虑大气随海拔升高而变稀薄非常重要。

**采样瑞利密度并累积光学深度**

```glsl
const float RAYLEIGH_SCALE_HEIGHT = 8.0; // km
const float ATMOSPHERE_HEIGHT = 100.0; // km - 卡门线
const float VIEW_DISTANCE = 200.0; // km
const int PRIMARY_STEPS = 24;
```
items.article_title_extracted: On Rendering the Sky, Sunsets, and Planets - The Blog of Maxime Heckel
items.description: This article explores how to render realistic skies and atmospheres in real time in the browser with shaders, from simple sky domes, to entire planets using shaders, raymarching, Rayleigh and Mie scattering, and ozone absorption.
items.description_zh: 本文探讨了如何在浏览器中利用着色器实时渲染逼真的天空与大气效果，内容涵盖从简单的天空穹顶到完整行星的构建，涉及着色器、光线步进、瑞利散射、米氏散射以及臭氧吸收等核心技术。
items.title: On Rendering the Sky, Sunsets, and Planets - The Blog of Maxime Heckel
items.title_zh: 关于渲染天空、日落与行星——Maxime Heckel 的博客

items.article_text:
As Copilot steadily evolves into a thought partner—an intelligent presence woven into your workflow—its backbone will become the Copilot Design System, an AI-forward design system we’re crafting to feel intentional and humane.
Design systems have always been living blueprints but in today’s era of AI-first product making, they don’t just consider interface design. They orchestrate coherence across complex systems, codify how to shape model behavior with a human-centric lens, and introduce new user behaviors and mental models rooted in AI’s ability to recognize and support human intent.
From orchestration patterns to iconography, the experience we’re building will ultimately have components that work together to amplify thinking, guide decisions, and unlock creativity—seamlessly, wherever you work. Anchored in customer feedback around creating better experiences, a fundamental question guides our system’s evolution: how would a thoughtful partner look and behave?
01
The system that shapes the system
Microsoft 365 apps have shaped organizational behavior for decades, establishing design patterns and mental models that billions of people rely on every day. Through that work, we’ve learned that productivity isn’t just a function of tools; it’s shaped by the broader system people think and work within. We can now bring these dimensions together through Copilot, supporting richer forms of collaboration and creativity across human-human, human-AI, and human-AI-human interactions.
How we design that partnership will influence how productivity unfolds across organizations big and small, today and ten years from now. Our design philosophy was to build at the speed of life, not AI hype—co-creating with customers and embedding user research into every step of the process.
02
Architecting Copilot across the Office apps
As we begin moving toward an AI-first design system, an early but essential part of that story is how Copilot shows up across Office apps. Four key architectural elements currently comprise the in-app system, designed to work how humans think. Cognition isn’t a fixed state. It’s a dynamic movement between exploration and focus, a lens continuously zooming in and out. These two movements—outward to discover, inward to refine—cycle continuously, shaping how we understand and act on the world. If intelligence doesn’t meet you at the right level of that cycle, it doesn’t feel like a partner. It feels like an interruption.
That insight is the structural foundation of how Copilot behaves across Office apps. It sees what you’re working on and understands the context, allowing it to act within the given environment. We implemented this through four architectural elements that operate at different cognitive levels:
An always-available, in-app Copilot that is contextually adaptive and a primary entry point to Chat.
03
One Copilot, wherever you work
Human behavior is deeply contextual and nuanced—how something is said or done often matters just as much as what. People make sense of the world as they physically and mentally move through it, including the liminal spaces between more defined moments. Associated words, feelings, experiences, and thoughts flow alongside us—but intelligence without continuity can feel like an interruption instead of a thought partner. Changing apps or surfaces can be like drifting out of the current. You should be able to invoke Copilot every time you move through your work.
Collaborative cognition draws from past conversations to understand context, recognize patterns, and change direction. That means the same Copilot you interacted with in Word should be the same one you converse with in Excel. Across M365, DAB serves as your go-to thought partner across desktop, web, iPad, and mobile.
On each in-app surface, DAB recognizes its immediate local context. It’s how Copilot consistently follows your train of thought, offering relevant and actionable suggestions while keeping your conversation continuous across
items.article_text_zh:
随着 Copilot 稳步演变为一个思维伙伴——一个融入你工作流程的智能存在——其核心支柱将是 Copilot 设计系统，这是我们精心打造的一个以 AI 为导向的设计系统，旨在带来有意识且人性化的体验。

设计系统一直是活生生的蓝图，但在当今以 AI 为先的产品制造时代，它们不仅考虑界面设计。它们协调复杂系统间的一致性，规范如何以人为中心的角度塑造模型行为，并引入植根于 AI 识别和支持人类意图能力的新用户行为和心理模型。

从编排模式到图标设计，我们正在构建的体验最终将拥有协同工作的组件，以增强思考、指导决策并释放创造力——无缝地，在你工作的任何地方。以围绕创造更好体验的客户反馈为基础，一个基本问题指导着我们系统的演进：一个体贴的伙伴会如何表现和行动？

**01**
**塑造系统的系统**

数十年来，Microsoft 365 应用塑造了组织行为，建立了数十亿人每天依赖的设计模式和心理模型。通过这项工作，我们了解到生产力不仅仅是工具的功能；它是由人们思考和工作的更广泛系统所塑造的。我们现在可以通过 Copilot 将这些维度结合起来，支持跨人际、人机以及人-机-人交互的更丰富的协作与创造力形式。

我们如何设计这种伙伴关系，将影响生产力如何在大小组织中展开，无论是今天还是十年后。我们的设计理念是，以生活的速度而非 AI 炒作的速度来构建——与客户共同创造，并将用户研究嵌入到流程的每一步。

**02**
**跨 Office 应用架构 Copilot**

随着我们开始迈向以 AI 为先的设计系统，这个故事中一个早期但关键的部分是 Copilot 如何在 Office 应用中呈现。目前，应用内系统由四个关键架构元素组成，其设计旨在匹配人类的思考方式。认知并非固定状态。它是探索与专注之间的动态运动，是一个持续拉近拉远的镜头。这两种运动——向外探索以发现，向内聚焦以精炼——循环往复，塑造我们理解和作用于世界的方式。如果智能未能在该循环的正确层级与你相遇，它就不会感觉像伙伴，而更像是一种打扰。

这一洞察是 Copilot 在 Office 应用中行为方式的结构基础。它能查看你正在处理的内容并理解上下文，从而在给定环境中采取行动。我们通过四个在不同认知层级运作的架构元素实现了这一点：

一个始终可用、上下文自适应且作为聊天主要入口点的应用内 Copilot。

**03**
**一个 Copilot，无论你在何处工作**

人类行为具有深刻的上下文相关性和细微差别——事情如何被说或做，往往与其内容同样重要。人们在物理和心理上穿越世界时理解世界，包括那些介于更明确时刻之间的阈限空间。相关的词语、感受、经历和想法与我们相伴而行——但缺乏连续性的智能可能感觉像打扰，而非思维伙伴。切换应用或界面可能如同偏离了当前思路。你应该能够在每次处理工作时调用 Copilot。

协作认知借鉴过去的对话来理解上下文、识别模式并改变方向。这意味着你在 Word 中交互过的同一个 Copilot，也应该是你在 Excel 中与之对话的那个。在 M365 中，DAB 作为你跨桌面、网页、iPad 和移动设备的首选思维伙伴。

在每个应用内界面上，DAB 都能识别其直接的本地上下文。这就是 Copilot 如何持续跟随你的思路，提供相关且可操作的建议，同时保持你的对话跨应用连续不断的方式。

as markdown Agent view in Claude Code Category Product announcements Product Claude Code Date May 11, 2026 Reading time 5 min Share Copy link https://claude.com/blog/agent-view-in-claude-code Today we're introducing agent view in Claude Code: one place to manage all your Claude Code sessions. When running agents in parallel before, you've probably had to manage multiple terminal tabs, a tmux grid, and an overloaded mental ledger of what you need to tackle next. With agent view in Claude Code, you can kick off new agents, send them to the background, and jump in only when Claude needs you. See at a glance which agents are waiting on you, which are still working, and which are done, so you can easily steer many all at once. How it works Agent view improves visualizing and interacting with your Claude Code sessions in the CLI. See everything at once Press the left arrow from any session or run claude agents from the terminal to open agent view. Each row shows the session, whether it needs your input, the contents of its last response, and when you last interacted with it. Peek and reply without leaving Select a session to peek at the last turn. If a session is waiting on a decision, answer inline and the session picks back up. Press enter to attach directly to sessions where you want to explore the full transcript. Background anything Lastly, users can take any existing session and add it to agent view using /bg or skip the foreground entirely using claude --bg [task] to launch a fresh session. How developers are using agent view A few patterns we have seen from early users: Scaling the number of concurrent sessions: Dispatch several ideas at once, each optionally paired with a skill, and return to a list of pull requests ready for review. Manage long running agents: PR babysitters, dashboard updaters, and other looping jobs show their next run time right in the list. Navigate between separate sessions: When you’re in the middle of a session, press the left arrow, start a related task or quick codebase question, then arrow right back into what you were doing. Peek shows the answer when it lands. See what shipped: Status indicators on each row plus the title in peek make it easy to scan which sessions produced

Meet Claude Products Claude Claude Code Claude Cowork Claude Security Features Claude for Chrome Claude for Slack Claude for Microsoft 365 Skills Models Opus Sonnet Haiku Platform Overview Developer docs Pricing Console login Solutions Use cases AI agents Coding Departments Legal Security Industries Customer support Education Financial services Government Healthcare Life sciences Nonprofits Pricing Overview API Plans Pro Max Team Enterprise Resources Insights Blog Customer stories Anthropic news Learn Anthropic Academy Courses Tutorials Use cases Tools Connectors Plugins Connect Events Community Login Contact sales Contact sales Contact sales Try Claude Try Claude Try Claude Contact sales Contact sales Contact sales Try Claude Try Claude Try Claude Contact sales Contact sales Contact sales Try Claude Try Claude Try Claude Contact sales Contact sales Contact sales Try Claude Try Claude Try Claude Meet Claude Products Claude Claude Code Claude Cowork Claude Security Features Claude for Chrome Claude for Slack Claude for Microsoft 365 Skills Models Opus Sonnet Haiku Platform Overview Developer docs Pricing Console login Solutions Use cases AI agents Coding Departments Legal Security Industries Customer support Education Financial services Government Healthcare Life sciences Nonprofits Pricing Overview API Plans Pro Max Team Enterprise Resources Insights Blog Customer stories Anthropic news Learn Anthropic Academy Courses Tutorials Use cases Tools Connectors Plugins Connect Events Community Login Contact sales Contact sales Contact sales Try Claude Try Claude Try Claude Contact sales Contact sales Contact sales Try Claude Try Claude Try Claude Blog Blog / Agent view in Claude Code Explore here Ask questions about this page Copy as markdown Agent view in Claude Code Category Product announcements Product Claude Code Date May 11, 2026 Reading time 5 min Share Copy link https://claude.com/blog/agent-view-in-claude-code

今天，我们推出 Claude Code 中的 agent view（代理视图）：一个管理所有 Claude Code 会话的统一界面。以前并行运行代理时，你可能不得不在多个终端标签页、tmux 网格和超负荷的脑内任务清单之间来回切换。有了 Claude Code 的 agent view，你可以启动新代理、将其发送到后台，并且仅在 Claude 需要你时再介入。一眼就能看出哪些代理在等待你、哪些仍在工作、哪些已完成，从而轻松同时指挥多个代理。

## 工作原理

Agent view 改进了在 CLI 中可视化和交互 Claude Code 会话的方式。

### 一览无余

从任意会话按左箭头键，或在终端中运行 `claude agents`，即可打开 agent view。每一行显示会话名称、是否需要你的输入、上次响应的内容，以及你上次与之交互的时间。

### 无需离开即可预览并回复

选择一个会话即可预览最后一轮对话。如果某个会话正在等待决策，直接内联回复，会话便会继续运行。按回车键可直接附加到你想查看完整对话记录的会话中。

### 将任何任务放入后台

最后，用户可以使用 `/bg` 将任意现有会话添加到 agent view，或者完全跳过前台，使用 `claude --bg [task]` 启动一个新会话。

## 开发者如何使用 agent view

以下是早期用户的一些使用模式：

- **扩展并发会话数量**：同时分派多个想法，每个可选配一个 skill（技能），然后返回查看已准备好审查的 PR 列表。
- **管理长时间运行的代理**：PR 监控、仪表盘更新等循环任务会在列表中直接显示下次运行时间。
- **在独立会话间导航**：当你在某个会话中时，按左箭头键启动一个相关任务或快速代码库问题，然后按右箭头键回到之前的工作。预览会在答案到达时显示。
- **查看已交付的内容**：每行的状态指示器加上预览中的标题，让你轻松扫描哪些会话产生了成果。

Daybreak | OpenAI for cybersecurity | OpenAI

# Daybreak

Frontier AI for cyber defenders.

[Request vulnerability scan](https://openai.com/daybreak/daybreak/request-a-vulnerability-scan/)[Contact sales](https://openai.com/daybreak/contact-sales/)

## Safer software, resilient by design

OpenAI Daybreak is our vision to change the way software is built and defended.

Daybreak is the first glimpse of sunlight in the morning. For cyber defense, it means seeing risk earlier, acting sooner, and helping make software resilient by design.

It starts from the premise that the next era of cyber defense should be built into software from the beginning by not only finding and patching vulnerabilities, but being resilient to them by design.

AI can now help defenders reason across codebases, identify subtle vulnerabilities, validate fixes, analyze unfamiliar systems, and move from discovery to remediation faster. Because those same capabilities can be misused, Daybreak pairs expanded defensive capability with trust, verification, proportional safeguards, and accountability.

The goal is simple: accelerate cyber defenders and continuously secure software.

Daybreak combines the intelligence of OpenAI models, the extensibility of Codex as an agentic harness, and our partners across the security flywheel to help make the world safer for everyone. Defenders can bring secure code review, threat modeling, patch validation, dependency risk analysis, detection, and remediation guidance into the everyday development loop so software becomes more resilient from the start.

In the coming weeks, we’re working with our industry and government partners as we prepare to deploy increasingly more cyber-capable models as part of our approach to iterative deployment.

## How to deploy AI in modern cyber defense

![icon of a bounding box](https://images.ctfassets.net/kftzwdyauwt9/6CXe1iCJZqYplgOK4yeVGL/fe031eb3987662421a5509301224d98a/bounds.svg?w=3840&q=90)

#### Focus on the threats that matter

Prioritize high-impact issues and reduce hours of analysis to minutes—with more efficient token usage.

![icon of a shield](https://images.ctfassets.net/kftzwdyauwt9/4jiWnWWSMBeqMwSJANlghs/6be7b78323c0bdc907284d0c05e52734/shield.svg?w=3840&q=90)

#### Patch safely, at scale

Generate and test patches directly in your repositories, with scoped access, monitoring, and review.

![Icon of a circle with a checkmark inside](https://images.ctfassets.net/kftzwdyauwt9/7u2dLcotT8lgGjmRSrHlaI/a4df05387cfae41a801f5a953115ba7c/checkmark-circle.svg?w=3840&q=90)

#### Verify every fix

Send results and audit-ready evidence back to your systems to track and verify remediation.

## Deploy frontier cyber intelligence inside Codex Security

### Find and fix vulnerabilities

Codex Security builds an editable threat model from your repository, then focuses analysis on realistic attack paths and high-impact code.

### Burn down the backlog

Validate likely vulnerabilities in an isolated environment, so teams can prioritize real, reproducible issues over noisy alerts.

### Automate detection and response

Use AI to spot higher risk vulnerabilities and automate monitoring for them end-to-end.

## Choose the right level of access

Contact the OpenAI team to align on the best model for your security workflows.

* [Request a vulnerability scan](https://openai.com/daybreak/daybreak/request-a-vulnerability-scan/)

items.article_text_zh:
# Daybreak

面向网络防御者的前沿AI。

[请求漏洞扫描](https://openai.com/daybreak/daybreak/request-a-vulnerability-scan/)[联系销售](https://openai.com/daybreak/contact-sales/)

## 更安全的软件，天生具备韧性

OpenAI Daybreak 是我们改变软件构建与防御方式的愿景。

Daybreak 寓意清晨的第一缕曙光。对于网络防御而言，它意味着更早发现风险、更快采取行动，并助力打造天生具备韧性的软件。

其核心理念是：下一代网络防御应从软件开发之初就融入其中，不仅要发现和修补漏洞，更要通过设计实现对这些漏洞的韧性。

如今，AI 能够帮助防御者跨代码库进行推理、识别细微漏洞、验证修复方案、分析陌生系统，并从发现漏洞到修复完成实现更快的闭环。由于同样的能力也可能被滥用，Daybreak 在扩展防御能力的同时，也配套了信任机制、验证流程、适度防护和问责制度。

目标很简单：加速网络防御者，并持续保障软件安全。

Daybreak 融合了 OpenAI 模型的智能、Codex 作为 agentic harness 的可扩展性，以及我们在安全飞轮中的合作伙伴，共同助力让世界更安全。防御者可以将安全代码审查、威胁建模、补丁验证、依赖风险分析、检测与修复指导融入日常开发流程，使软件从一开始就更具韧性。

未来几周，我们将与行业及政府合作伙伴协作，准备部署能力日益增强的网络模型，这是我们迭代部署策略的一部分。

## 如何在现代网络防御中部署 AI

![边界框图标](https://images.ctfassets.net/kftzwdyauwt9/6CXe1iCJZqYplgOK4yeVGL/fe031eb3987662421a5509301224d98a/bounds.svg?w=3840&q=90)

#### 聚焦真正重要的威胁

优先处理高影响问题，将数小时的分析缩短至数分钟——同时实现更高效的 token 使用。

![盾牌图标](https://images.ctfassets.net/kftzwdyauwt9/4jiWnWWSMBeqMwSJANlghs/6be7b78323c0bdc907284d0c05e52734/shield.svg?w=3840&q=90)

#### 安全地大规模打补丁

直接在代码仓库中生成并测试补丁，并配有范围限定访问、监控和审查。

![带对勾的圆形图标](https://images.ctfassets.net/kftzwdyauwt9/7u2dLcotT8lgGjmRSrHlaI/a4df05387cfae41a801f5a953115ba7c/checkmark-circle.svg?w=3840&q=90)

#### 验证每一项修复

将结果和可审计的证据发回您的系统，以跟踪和验证修复情况。

## 在 Codex Security 中部署前沿网络智能

### 发现并修复漏洞

Codex Security 从您的代码仓库构建可编辑的威胁模型，然后聚焦于现实攻击路径和高影响代码进行分析。

### 清理积压任务

在隔离环境中验证潜在漏洞，使团队能够优先处理真实、可复现的问题，而非噪音警报。

### 自动化检测与响应

利用 AI 识别高风险漏洞，并端到端地自动监控它们。

## 选择适当的访问级别

联系 OpenAI 团队，为您的安全工作流确定最佳模型。

* [请求漏洞扫描](https://openai.com/daybreak/daybreak/request-a-vulnerability-scan/)

items.article_text:
Today, we’re announcing a research preview of interaction models: models that handle interaction natively rather than through external scaffolding. We think interactivity should scale alongside intelligence; the way we work with AI should not be treated as an afterthought. Interaction models let people collaborate with AI the way we naturally collaborate with each other—they continuously take in audio, video, and text, and think, respond, and act in real time.
We train an interaction model from scratch. To ensure real-time responsiveness, we adopt a multi-stream, micro-turn design. Our research preview demonstrates qualitatively new interaction capabilities, as well as state-of-the-art combined performance in intelligence and responsiveness.
The collaboration bottleneck
AI labs often treat the ability for AI to work autonomously as the model’s most important capability.Kwa, T., West, B., Becker, J., et al. Measuring AI Ability to Complete Long Tasks. [METR](https://metr.org/blog/2025-03-19-measuring-ai-ability-to-complete-long-tasks/), 2025. As a result, today’s models and interfaces aren’t optimized for humans to remain in the loop.A [recent frontier model card](https://www-cdn.anthropic.com/8b8380204f74670be75e81c820ca8dda846ab289.pdf) states: “Importantly, we find that when used in an interactive, synchronous, “hands-on-keyboard” pattern, the benefits of the model were less clear. When used in this fashion, some users perceived [our model] as too slow and did not realize as much value. Autonomous, long-running agent harnesses better elicited the model’s coding capabilities.”
Autonomous interfaces are valuable, but in most real work, users can’t fully specify their requirements upfront and walk away—good results benefit from a collaborative process where the human stays in the loop, clarifying and giving feedback along the way. However, humans increasingly get pushed out not because the work doesn’t need them, but because the interface has no room for them. Instead, people are most effective when they can collaborate with AI the same way we do with other people: messaging, talking, listening, seeing, showing, and interjecting as needed—and for the model to do the same.Communication gets better with: (a) Copresence: people can interact with what others are interacting with; (b) Contemporality: people receive information as it’s produced by others with instant feedback; (c) Simultaneity: people receive and produce information at the same time. Clark H. and Brennan S., “Grounding in Communication,” in Perspectives on Socially Shared Cognition, 1991., The evanescence of orality for its participatory (cf. objectively distanced) nature. Today’s computers and mediums of knowledge work have similar interactive properties. Ong, W. J.. In Orality and Literacy: The technologizing of the word, 1982.
In order to resolve this, we need to move beyond the current turn-based interface for the models. Today’s models experience reality in a single thread.We are referring to commercial general-purpose frontier models—there are smaller-scale or specialized models like Moshi, PersonaPlex, Nemotron VoiceChat, or GPT-Realtime-Translate. Until the user finishes typing or speaking, the model waits with no perception of what the user is doing or how the user is doing it. Until the model finishes generating, its perception freezes, receiving no new information until it finishes or is interrupted. This creates a narrow channel for human-AI collaboration that limits how much of a person’s knowledge,“Metis, with the premium it places on practical knowledge, experience, and stochastic reasoning…is the mode of reasoning most appropriate to complex material and social tasks where the uncertainties are so daunting that we must trust our (experienced) intuition and feel our way.” Scott, J. C: Métis. In Seeing like a State: How certain schemes to improve the human condition have failed, 1998., “A little reflection will show that there is…a body of very importan
items.article_text_zh:
今天，我们宣布一项关于交互模型的研究预览：这些模型原生处理交互，而非通过外部脚手架实现。我们认为交互性应随智能同步扩展；我们与AI协作的方式不应被视为事后补充。交互模型让人们能够以自然协作的方式与AI合作——它们持续接收音频、视频和文本，并实时思考、回应和行动。

我们从零开始训练一个交互模型。为确保实时响应，我们采用了多流、微轮次设计。我们的研究预览展示了定性的新型交互能力，以及在智能和响应性方面的最先进综合性能。

**协作瓶颈**

AI实验室常将AI自主工作的能力视为模型最重要的能力。Kwa, T., West, B., Becker, J., 等. 《衡量AI完成长任务的能力》.[METR](https://metr.org/blog/2025-03-19-measuring-ai-ability-to-complete-long-tasks/)，2025年。结果，当今的模型和界面并未针对人类保持参与进行优化。一份[近期前沿模型卡](https://www-cdn.anthropic.com/8b8380204f74670be75e81c820ca8dda846ab289.pdf)指出：“重要的是，我们发现当以交互式、同步、‘手不离键盘’的模式使用时，模型的好处不太明显。以这种方式使用时，一些用户认为[我们的模型]太慢，未能实现同等价值。自主、长期运行的代理框架能更好地激发模型的编码能力。”

自主界面很有价值，但在大多数实际工作中，用户无法完全预先指定需求然后离开——好的结果受益于协作过程，其中人类保持参与，在过程中澄清和提供反馈。然而，人类被逐渐排除在外，并非因为工作不需要他们，而是因为界面没有容纳他们的空间。相反，当人们能够像与他人协作一样与AI协作时，他们最为高效：根据需要发消息、交谈、倾听、观察、展示和插话——模型也应如此。沟通通过以下方式得到改善：(a) 共在：人们可以与他人正在交互的内容互动；(b) 共时：人们能即时接收他人产生的信息并获得即时反馈；(c) 同时性：人们能同时接收和产生信息。Clark H. 和 Brennan S.，《沟通中的基础》，载于《社会共享认知视角》，1991年。口头表达的短暂性源于其参与性（相对于客观距离）本质。当今的计算机和知识工作媒介具有类似的交互特性。Ong, W. J.，《口语与书面：词语的技术化》，1982年。

为解决这一问题，我们需要超越当前基于轮次的模型界面。当今的模型在单一线程中体验现实。我们指的是商业通用前沿模型——存在较小规模或专门模型，如Moshi、PersonaPlex、Nemotron VoiceChat或GPT-Realtime-Translate。在用户完成打字或说话之前，模型等待，对用户正在做什么或如何做毫无感知。在模型完成生成之前，其感知冻结，直到完成或被中断才接收新信息。这为人类-AI协作创造了一个狭窄通道，限制了人的知识、“Metis强调实践知识、经验和随机推理……是最适合复杂物质和社会任务的推理模式，其中不确定性如此之大，我们必须信任（经验丰富的）直觉并摸索前进。”Scott, J. C.，《Metis》，载于《像国家一样看待：某些改善人类状况的方案为何失败》，1998年。“稍加反思就会发现……存在一个非常重要的

items.article_text: Skip to main content Redraw Docs Playground Redraw You are renderful Documentation Variable Strokes Stroke width and colors are TypeScript functions that are compiled and executed on GPU. They receive the path geometry information as input giving you full control to create dynamic, geometry-aware strokes. Variable strokes Open in editor → Eleven-color palette interpolated along the path Open in editor → Color Along Path Like for strokes, colors are TypeScript functions compiled and executed on GPU. You have full control of the color of fills and strokes based on the geometry of the path. Vector Feathering Soft, distance-based blur computed directly from vector geometry. No rasterization, no post-process pass, just feathered layers composed in a single shader. Frosted glass: feathered layer, tint, and grain over a colorful backdrop Open in editor → Flat UI elements with dynamic highlights and reflections based on the geometry of the path Open in editor → Physically Based Rendering Bring lighting, Fresnel, and GGX specular straight into 2D paths. Strokes and shapes pick up materials like ceramic, metal, or glass without leaving the vector pipeline. Resources Documentation Made with ❤️ in beautiful Zürich Switzerland
items.article_text_zh:
Redraw 文档 演示 Redraw

您即渲染力

**可变描边**

描边宽度和颜色是 TypeScript 函数，在 GPU 上编译并执行。它们接收路径几何信息作为输入，让您完全掌控创建动态、几何感知的描边。

可变描边 → 在编辑器中打开

沿路径插值的十一色调色板 → 在编辑器中打开

**沿路径着色**

与描边类似，颜色也是 TypeScript 函数，在 GPU 上编译并执行。您可以根据路径几何信息完全控制填充和描边的颜色。

**矢量羽化**

基于距离的柔和模糊，直接从矢量几何计算。无需光栅化，无需后处理通道，仅在一个着色器中合成羽化图层。

毛玻璃效果：羽化图层、色调和纹理叠加在彩色背景上 → 在编辑器中打开

基于路径几何的扁平 UI 元素，带有动态高光和反射 → 在编辑器中打开

**基于物理的渲染**

将光照、菲涅尔效应和 GGX 镜面高光直接引入 2D 路径。描边和形状可呈现陶瓷、金属或玻璃等材质，无需脱离矢量管线。

资源 文档

items.article_title_extracted: SDocs
items.description: SDocs is an open-source tool designed to enhance the readability, sharing, and privacy of Markdown (.md) files. By using the command `sdoc`, users can display `.md` files styled in a browser without sending data to a server; the content is encoded in the URL's hash, maintaining privacy. It features easy sharing via links, customizable styles through YAML front matter, and support for multiple outputs including PDF and Word documents. Users can work offline, utilize drag-and-drop functionality, copy comments directly, and generate short links while ensuring security through end-to-end encryption. The platform emphasizes user-friendly features like comment modes and chart integration for enhanced presentations.
items.description_zh: SDocs 是一款开源工具，旨在提升 Markdown（.md）文件的可读性、分享便捷性和隐私保护。用户通过 `sdoc` 命令，可在浏览器中展示经过样式渲染的 `.md` 文件，且无需将数据发送至服务器——内容被编码在 URL 的哈希值中，从而保障隐私。该工具支持通过链接轻松分享、通过 YAML 前置元数据自定义样式，并支持输出 PDF 和 Word 文档等多种格式。用户可离线使用、利用拖拽功能、直接复制评论，并通过端到端加密生成短链接，确保安全性。平台强调用户友好特性，如评论模式和图表集成，以增强演示效果。
items.title: SDocs

items.article_text:
Japan's invisible electric wall
7 min read May 9, 2026
Looking for vintage clocks
Japan’s [mains electricity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mains_electricity)
comes in two different frequencies with half of the country using 50 Hz and the
other 60 Hz.
I stumbled onto this while shopping for vintage desk clocks on eBay. I already
knew about the voltage difference — Japan runs on 100 V, the US on 120 V,
same-looking plugs notwithstanding. Back when I tried to buy a
[Muji](https://arun.is/blog/muji-books/) toaster oven, I stumbled upon reviews of the brave
souls who learned the hard way why you shouldn’t import one. They talked about
how the low setting was pretty much high, and the highest setting would
immediately burn its contents.
This isn’t a problem with most low-power electronics, since they use USB-C or a power brick that supports a wide range of frequencies and voltages and converts it to the right DC voltage. So while I was looking for clocks, I found plenty that would work in the US, like this Copal 222 that can do anywhere from 80 to 120 V at 60 Hz.
But then I noticed something weird. First up is this Seiko flip clock shaped like a wooden ball. On the back it says it takes 100 V at 50 Hz, with a warning to check the mains frequency when using it.
Then I find another Seiko flip clock with a switch on the back between 50 Hz and 60 Hz. Is that switch for using in a different country?
I remember in older PC power supplies there was often
[a switch between 120 V and 240 V](https://www.lifewire.com/power-supply-voltage-switch-2625973),
but I don’t remember seeing a switch for the frequency.
The electric wall
I looked at the
[table of mains voltages, frequencies, and plugs](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mains_electricity_by_country#Table_of_mains_voltages,_frequencies,_and_plugs)
on Wikipedia and discovered that not only is Japan the only country that uses
100 V, but that its supply frequency is both 50 Hz and 60 Hz.
I became more curious when I looked at a map of Japan and noticed that the western half uses 60 Hz while the eastern half is on 50 Hz, with a border in the middle where some places seem to use both.
Marveling at railfans’ maps
Coincidentally, I bumped into the invisible wall again when I was browsing
[OpenRailwayMap](https://www.openrailwaymap.org/), a collaborative project based
on OpenStreetMap technology aimed at mapping railroad lines worldwide. Looking
around Japan, I noticed a couple of things.
If you follow the
[Tōkaidō Shinkansen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tokaido_Shinkansen) (bullet
train) from Tokyo towards Osaka, you’ll notice all the conventional rail lines
that generally use DC power. But you’ll also see the
[New Transit Yurikamome](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yurikamome) running
nearby — and that one runs on 50 Hz, while the Tōkaidō Shinkansen runs on 60 Hz.
50 Hz is expected, since that’s the mains frequency in Tokyo.
You see, when the first Shinkansen was constructed, given that most of the route
was in the 60 Hz section of the country, the portions that fell in the 50 Hz
section had frequency conversion
[substations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Substation) installed to standardize
the entire line at 60 Hz.[[1]](https://arun.is#footnote_1) They’ve
continued to upgrade these converters for efficiency and reliability till today.
But the 50 Hz/60 Hz divide doesn’t stop with the Tōkaidō Shinkansen. It gets
more interesting with the
[Hokuriku Shinkansen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hokuriku_Shinkansen), which
[we recently took from Tokyo to Kanazawa](https://arun.is/blog/kanazawa-1/). When that line
opened in 1997, it was known as the Nagano Shinkansen — helpful since it
terminated in Nagano and would take tourists to the Nagano Olympics. It was the
first Shinkansen to have trains (the
[E2 series](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/E2_Series_Shinkansen)) capable of
operating on both 50 Hz and 60 Hz overhead lines because reaching Nagano
required crossing that boundary.

items.article_text_zh:
# 日本无形的电力墙  
7分钟阅读 · 2026年5月9日  

## 寻找复古时钟  

日本的[市电](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mains_electricity)有两种不同的频率，全国一半地区使用50赫兹，另一半使用60赫兹。  

我在eBay上淘复古座钟时偶然发现了这一点。我早就知道电压差异——日本用100伏，美国用120伏，尽管插头看起来一样。以前我试图买一台[Muji](https://arun.is/blog/muji-books/)烤面包机时，看到了一些勇敢者的评论，他们用惨痛教训明白了为什么不该进口这种电器。他们提到，低温档基本相当于高温档，而最高档会立刻烤焦里面的食物。  

对于大多数低功率电子产品来说，这不是问题，因为它们使用USB-C或支持宽频率和电压范围的电源适配器，并将其转换为正确的直流电压。所以在我找时钟时，发现很多都能在美国使用，比如这款Copal 222，支持80到120伏、60赫兹。  

但随后我注意到一些奇怪的地方。首先是这款Seiko翻页钟，外形像个木球。背面写着它需要100伏、50赫兹，并警告使用时需检查市电频率。  

接着我又找到另一款Seiko翻页钟，背面有一个可在50赫兹和60赫兹之间切换的开关。这个开关是为了在不同国家使用吗？  

我记得老式PC电源上常有[一个120伏和240伏之间的切换开关](https://www.lifewire.com/power-supply-voltage-switch-2625973)，但我不记得见过频率切换开关。  

## 电力墙  

我查看了维基百科上的[市电电压、频率和插头对照表](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mains_electricity_by_country#Table_of_mains_voltages,_frequencies,_and_plugs)，发现日本不仅是唯一使用100伏的国家，而且其供电频率同时包含50赫兹和60赫兹。  

当我查看日本地图时更加好奇了：西半部使用60赫兹，东半部使用50赫兹，中间有一条分界线，有些地方似乎两种频率都用。  

## 惊叹于铁路迷的地图  

巧合的是，我在浏览[OpenRailwayMap](https://www.openrailwaymap.org/)时再次撞上了这堵无形的墙。这是一个基于OpenStreetMap技术的协作项目，旨在绘制全球铁路线路。查看日本时，我注意到了几件事。  

如果你沿着[东海道新干线](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tokaido_Shinkansen)（子弹头列车）从东京前往大阪，你会发现所有常规铁路线通常使用直流电。但你也会看到附近的[新交通百合海鸥线](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yurikamome)——它运行在50赫兹下，而东海道新干线运行在60赫兹下。50赫兹是意料之中的，因为那是东京的市电频率。  

你看，当第一条新干线修建时，由于大部分线路位于日本的60赫兹区域，落在50赫兹区域的部分安装了[变频变电站](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Substation)，以将整条线路统一为60赫兹。[[1]](https://arun.is#footnote_1) 直到今天，他们仍在不断升级这些变频器以提高效率和可靠性。  

但50赫兹/60赫兹的分界并不止于东海道新干线。更令人感兴趣的是[北陆新干线](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hokuriku_Shinkansen)，[我们最近刚乘坐它从东京前往金泽](https://arun.is/blog/kanazawa-1/)。这条线路于1997年开通时被称为长野新干线——这很实用，因为它终点在长野，能将游客送往长野冬奥会。它是第一条拥有列车（[E2系列](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/E2_Series_Shinkansen)）能在50赫兹和60赫兹接触网下运行的新干线，因为要到达长野必须跨越那条分界线。  

[E4双层列车](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/E4_Series_Shinkansen)

items.article_text: Skip to main content Learn how to Mitigate supply chain attacks with pnpm pnpm Docs Blog FAQ Benchmarks Community More Blog FAQ Community Benchmarks 11.x Next 11.x 10.x English English Italiano (61%) 简体中文 (99%) 日本語 (86%) 한국어 (52%) Português Brasileiro (61%) 正體中文 (43%) Русский (38%) Українська (80%) Français (50%) Türkçe (28%) Español (67%) Bahasa Indonesia (32%) Help Us Translate 🧡 Sponsor Us Open Collective GitHub Sponsors Crypto Donations Search Introduction Usage CLI commands Configuration Features Recipes Advanced Migrating from v10 to v11 Error Codes Logos Limitations Symlinked `node_modules` structure How peers are resolved Uninstalling pnpm pnpm vs npm Advanced Migrating from v10 to v11 Version: 11.x On this page Migrating from v10 to v11 pnpm v11 introduces several breaking changes to how configuration is read and which settings are available. Most config changes are mechanical and can be applied by a codemod; the remainder require human attention. pnpm prints a pointer to this page when pnpm self-update 11 is run from a v10 install. Run the codemod ​ cd /path/to/your/project pnpx codemod run pnpm-v10-to-v11 # or pnpm add --global codemod codemod run pnpm-v10-to-v11 The codemod applies the following automatically: Moves settings out of package.json#pnpm into pnpm-workspace.yaml . In v11, pnpm no longer reads configuration from the pnpm field in package.json . Splits .npmrc into auth/registry vs. everything else . v11 only reads auth and registry settings from .npmrc . Every other setting ( hoist-pattern , node-linker , save-exact , …) is moved into pnpm-workspace.yaml with a camelCase key. Per-subproject .npmrc files land under packageConfigs["<project-name>"] . Consolidates build-dependency settings into allowBuilds . onlyBuiltDependencies , neverBuiltDependencies , ignoredBuiltDependencies , and onlyBuiltDependenciesFile are merged into a single allowBuilds map ( { name: true | false } ). Replaces the package-manager strictness settings with pmOnFail . managePackageManagerVersions , packageManagerStrict , and packageManagerStrictVersion are collapsed into one pmOnFail: download | ignore | warn | error setting. Renames allowNonAppliedPatches → allowUnusedPatches , and auditConfig.ignoreCves → auditConfig.ignoreGhsas (the key is renamed; CVE IDs still need to be converted to GHSA IDs manually — see below). Converts useNodeVersion into a devEngines.runtime entry on the root package.json . Bumps packageManager in package.json to the target pnpm v11 version. Manual follow-ups ​ The following changes are not automatable and need human attention: CVE → GHSA . auditConfig.ignoreCves was renamed to auditConfig.ignoreGhsas . Replace each CVE-YYYY-NNNNN entry with the matching GHSA-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx ID (visible in the "More info" column of pnpm audit output). ignorePatchFailures has been removed. Failed patches now always throw; fix the patch or remove the dependency. executionEnv.nodeVersion in a workspace subpackage's package.json#pnpm is removed. Declare the runtime in that subpackage's devEngines.runtime instead. npm_config_* environment variables are no longer read. Rename them to pnpm_config_* wherever they are set (CI configs, shell profiles, Docker images). pnpm link <pkg-name> no longer resolves packages from the global store. Use a relative or absolute path ( pnpm link ./foo ). pnpm install -g (with no arguments) is no longer supported. Use pnpm add -g <pkg> instead. pnpm server has been removed with no replacement. Script names shadow built-in commands . If your package.json defines a script named clean , setup , deploy , or rebuild , pnpm <name> now runs the script instead of the built-in command. Use pnpm pm <name> to force the built-in. For the full list of breaking changes, see the v11 changelog . Edit this page Previous Working with Podman Next Error Codes Run the codemod Manual follow-ups Docs Getting Started pnpm CLI Workspace Settings (pnpm-workspace.yaml) Community X (Twitter) YouTube Reddit Bluesky Discord Con

了解如何通过 pnpm 缓解供应链攻击

pnpm 文档 博客 常见问题 基准测试 社区 更多

博客 常见问题 社区 基准测试

11.x 下一个版本 11.x 10.x

English English Italiano (61%) 简体中文 (99%) 日本語 (86%) 한국어 (52%) Português Brasileiro (61%) 正體中文 (43%) Русский (38%) Українська (80%) Français (50%) Türkçe (28%) Español (67%) Bahasa Indonesia (32%)

帮助我们翻译 🧡 赞助我们 Open Collective GitHub Sponsors 加密货币捐赠

搜索

使用入门 用法 CLI 命令 配置 功能 指南 高级 从 v10 迁移到 v11 错误代码 Logo 限制 符号链接的 `node_modules` 结构 如何解析对等依赖 卸载 pnpm pnpm vs npm

高级 从 v10 迁移到 v11

版本: 11.x

本页内容

从 v10 迁移到 v11

pnpm v11 在配置的读取方式和可用设置方面引入了多项重大变更。大多数配置变更是机械性的，可以通过 codemod 应用；其余部分需要人工处理。当从 v10 安装运行 `pnpm self-update 11` 时，pnpm 会打印指向此页面的链接。

运行 codemod
​
cd /path/to/your/project
pnpx codemod run pnpm-v10-to-v11
# 或者
pnpm add --global codemod
codemod run pnpm-v10-to-v11

codemod 会自动应用以下更改：

- 将设置从 `package.json#pnpm` 移至 `pnpm-workspace.yaml`。在 v11 中，pnpm 不再从 `package.json` 的 `pnpm` 字段读取配置。
- 将 `.npmrc` 拆分为认证/仓库设置和其他设置。v11 仅从 `.npmrc` 读取认证和仓库设置。所有其他设置（`hoist-pattern`、`node-linker`、`save-exact` 等）都移至 `pnpm-workspace.yaml`，并使用 camelCase 键名。每个子项目的 `.npmrc` 文件归入 `packageConfigs["<project-name>"]` 下。
- 将构建依赖设置合并为 `allowBuilds`。`onlyBuiltDependencies`、`neverBuiltDependencies`、`ignoredBuiltDependencies` 和 `onlyBuiltDependenciesFile` 合并为一个 `allowBuilds` 映射（`{ name: true | false }`）。
- 用 `pmOnFail` 替换包管理器严格性设置。`managePackageManagerVersions`、`packageManagerStrict` 和 `packageManagerStrictVersion` 合并为一个 `pmOnFail: download | ignore | warn | error` 设置。
- 将 `allowNonAppliedPatches` 重命名为 `allowUnusedPatches`，并将 `auditConfig.ignoreCves` 重命名为 `auditConfig.ignoreGhsas`（键名已重命名；CVE ID 仍需手动转换为 GHSA ID — 见下文）。
- 将 `useNodeVersion` 转换为根 `package.json` 上的 `devEngines.runtime` 条目。
- 将 `package.json` 中的 `packageManager` 升级到目标 pnpm v11 版本。

手动跟进
​
以下变更无法自动完成，需要人工处理：

- **CVE → GHSA**。`auditConfig.ignoreCves` 已重命名为 `auditConfig.ignoreGhsas`。将每个 `CVE-YYYY-NNNNN` 条目替换为对应的 `GHSA-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx` ID（可在 `pnpm audit` 输出的“更多信息”列中查看）。
- **`ignorePatchFailures` 已被移除**。失败的补丁现在始终会抛出错误；请修复补丁或移除依赖。
- **工作区子包的 `package.json#pnpm` 中的 `executionEnv.nodeVersion` 已被移除**。请在该子包的 `devEngines.runtime` 中声明运行时。
- **不再读取 `npm_config_*` 环境变量**。请将它们重命名为 `pnpm_config_*`（在 CI 配置、shell 配置文件、Docker 镜像中设置的地方）。
- **`pnpm link <pkg-name>` 不再从全局存储解析包**。请使用相对或绝对路径（`pnpm link ./foo`）。
- **不再支持 `pnpm install -g`（不带参数）**。请改用 `pnpm add -g <pkg>`。
- **`pnpm server` 已被移除，无替代方案**。
- **脚本名称会遮蔽内置命令**。如果你的 `package.json` 定义了名为 `clean`、`setup`、`deploy` 或 `rebuild` 的脚本，`pnpm <name>` 现在会运行该脚本而不是内置命令。请使用 `pnpm pm <name>` 来强制运行内置命令。

有关所有重大变更的完整列表，请参阅 v11 更新日志。

编辑此页面 上一页 使用 Podman 工作 下一页 错误代码

items.description:
Lump 是一个智能运动计数器，利用 AirPods 的运动传感器，精确记录跳绳、开合跳和深蹲等运动数据。
无需额外设备，让运动更简单、更智能。

【主要功能】
• 智能计数：自动识别并精确计数跳绳、开合跳和深蹲动作
• 多种模式：支持自由模式、计时模式和计数模式
• 目标设定：可设置运动时长或次数目标，达成时自动提醒
• 数据统计：记录每日运动数据，直观展示运动成果
• 热量计算：实时计算消耗的卡路里，追踪运动效果
• 无感佩戴：配合 AirPods 使用，无需额外设备

【使用场景】
• 居家健身：在家也能享受专业运动体验
• 办公健身：工作间隙轻松完成运动目标
• 室内训练：不受天气影响，随时随地健身

【贴心设计】
• 简洁界面：操作简单直观，零门槛上手
• 实时反馈：运动过程中实时展示数据
• 成就提醒：达成目标时及时提醒
• 数据记录：详细记录每次运动数据，方便回顾

让运动变得更简单、更智能，从 Lump 开始！

需要 iOS 15.0 或更高版本
支持 iPhone 和 AirPods

items.article_text:
TracingArt
Millions of Resources, Spanning Five Centuries
Here's how the Getty Provenance Index is transforming research on the social life of art.
[Still Life with a Chinese Porcelain Jar](https://collections.discovernewfields.org/art/artwork/57562), Willem Kalf
Gift of Mrs. James W. Fesler in memory of Daniel W. and Elizabeth C. Marmon,
Indianapolis Museum of Art at Newfields, [No copyright](https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/mark/1.0/)
The following year, Knoedler & Co. sold it to "Mrs. James W. Fesler," who donated it to the Indianapolis Museum of Art at Newfields the same year.
Records show her full name was Caroline Marmon Fesler.
Often disguised behind their husbands' names, female collectors played a major role in the formation of many American museum collections.
Studying an object's trajectory - from the moment it's created, then as it moves between new owners or custodians
and eventually is lost or destroyed, or reaches its present location - is the provenance researcher's job.
In 1669, Dutch artist Willem Kalf painted Still Life with a Chinese Porcelain Jar.
Kalf worked in 1600s Amsterdam, a dynamic trade center where luxurious imports - like the Chinese porcelain jar,
Venetian goblet, and Ottoman rug depicted in his painting - circulated widely, and were a sign of cosmopolitan taste.
For the next two centuries of its existence, it's hard to know much about how and where this painting traveled.
In 1864, it was sold at auction in Paris, but the name of the buyer remains unknown.
In 1936, it was part of an art exhibition in Amsterdam.
A label on the back of the painting shows it was lent by the art dealer Dr. H. Burg.
Sometime between 1936 and 1944, the painting traveled to New York with a couple from Cologne, Germany.
Ongoing [research](https://collections.discovernewfields.org/art/provenance-research) by the Indianapolis Museum of
Art at Newfields, where the painting is now,
seeks to clarify the movements of artworks during periods of conflict - such as World War II,
when many artworks were taken from their rightful owners by the Nazi regime.
In 1944, the famous art dealership Michael Knoedler & Co. purchased the painting.
1669
1700
1864
1936
1940
1944
The hundreds of thousands of transactions in
the newly reconfigured [Getty Provenance Index](https://www.getty.edu/research/provenance)
form a complex web of relationships between
objects, people, events, places and time.
Through it, we can see that painters acquired works from fellow artists, and often how much they paid for them.
In 1892, Claude Monet purchased
La Jatte de lait by Berthe Morisot at her first solo show. Both were active members of the social and artistic circles of the Impressionists in Paris.
Berthe Morisot
Claude Monet
[La Jatte de lait](https://www.sothebys.com/en/buy/auction/2022/modern-day-auction/la-jatte-de-lait-2), Berthe Morisot
Private Collection, Photograph Courtesy of Sotheby's, Inc. ©
Caravaggio
Giuseppe Cesari
[Boy with Basket of Fruit, Merisi Michelangelo](https://www.collezionegalleriaborghese.it/opere/giovane-con-canestra-di-frutta), called Caravaggio
Galleria Borghese, © Galleria Borghese / ph. Mauro Coen
[Self-Portrait as Bacchus (known as "Sick Bacchus")](https://www.collezionegalleriaborghese.it/en/opere/self-portrait-as-bacchus-known-as-sick-bacchus), Merisi Michelangelo, called Caravaggio
Galleria Borghese, © Galleria Borghese / ph. Mauro Coen
300 years earlier in Rome, artist Giuseppe Cesari was Caravaggio's first teacher, and obtained two of the artist's most famous paintings.
The paintings and others were seized- perhaps on trumped up charges- by Cardinal Scipione Borghese in 1607, and still reside in the Galleria Borghese to this day.
Paulus Potter
Adolphe Schloss
Jean-Baptiste Pierre Lebrun
Chevalier Lambert
André Coquille
William Norton
Baron Hastings
Charles Sedelmeyer
Years later, the painting was looted by the Vichy Government of France during WW2
[The "Piebald" Horse](https://www.getty.edu/art/collection/object
items.article_text_zh:
# 溯源艺术

数百万资源，横跨五个世纪
盖蒂溯源索引如何改变对艺术社会生命的研究

[《带中国瓷罐的静物》](https://collections.discovernewfields.org/art/artwork/57562)，威廉·卡尔夫
詹姆斯·W·费斯勒夫人捐赠，以纪念丹尼尔·W·和伊丽莎白·C·马蒙，
印第安纳波利斯艺术博物馆（纽菲尔德），[无版权](https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/mark/1.0/)

次年，诺德勒公司将其出售给"詹姆斯·W·费斯勒夫人"，她同年将其捐赠给印第安纳波利斯艺术博物馆（纽菲尔德）。
记录显示她的全名是卡罗琳·马蒙·费斯勒。
女性收藏家常常隐于丈夫姓名之后，却在许多美国博物馆馆藏的形成中扮演了重要角色。

研究一件物品的轨迹——从它被创作的那一刻起，到它在新的所有者或保管人之间流转，
最终遗失、毁坏或到达当前所在地——这就是溯源研究者的工作。

1669年，荷兰艺术家威廉·卡尔夫创作了《带中国瓷罐的静物》。
卡尔夫在17世纪的阿姆斯特丹工作，这是一个充满活力的贸易中心，画中描绘的中国瓷罐、
威尼斯高脚杯和奥斯曼地毯等奢华进口品广泛流通，是世界性品味的象征。

在其后的两个世纪里，这幅画如何、在何处流转，我们知之甚少。
1864年，它在巴黎的一场拍卖会上售出，但买家的姓名至今不详。
1936年，它成为阿姆斯特丹一场艺术展览的一部分。
画作背面的标签显示，它由艺术品经销商H·伯格博士借出。
1936年至1944年间，这幅画随一对来自德国科隆的夫妇来到纽约。

印第安纳波利斯艺术博物馆（纽菲尔德）正在进行中的[研究](https://collections.discovernewfields.org/art/provenance-research)（该画现藏于此）
旨在厘清冲突时期——如二战期间，纳粹政权从合法所有者手中夺走大量艺术品——艺术品的流转情况。

1944年，著名艺术品经销商迈克尔·诺德勒公司购得此画。

新近重构的[盖蒂溯源索引](https://www.getty.edu/research/provenance)中数十万条交易记录，
构成了物品、人物、事件、地点和时间之间复杂的关系网络。
通过它，我们可以看到画家从同行艺术家那里购得作品，以及他们通常支付的价格。

1892年，克劳德·莫奈在贝尔特·莫里索的首次个展上购得了她的《牛奶罐》。两人都是巴黎印象派社交和艺术圈中的活跃成员。

这些画作连同其他作品于1607年被枢机主教希皮奥内·博尔盖塞没收——可能基于捏造的指控——至今仍收藏在博尔盖塞美术馆。

# Advancing voice intelligence with new models in the API

A new generation of realtime voice models that can reason, translate, and transcribe as people speak.

00:0004:03

Listen to article

Share

We’re introducing three audio models in the API that unlock a new class of voice apps for developers. With these models, developers can build voice experiences that feel more natural, respond more intelligently, and take action in real time:

* **GPT‑Realtime‑2**, our first voice model with GPT‑5‑class reasoning that can handle harder requests and carry the conversation forward naturally.
* **GPT‑Realtime‑Translate**, a new live translation model that translates speech from 70+ input languages into 13 output languages while keeping pace with the speaker.
* **GPT‑Realtime‑Whisper**, a new streaming speech-to-text that transcribes speech live as the speaker talks.

# Try GPT-Realtime-2

Start the session, then talk naturally with GPT-Realtime-2.

Start sessionWhat can I ask?

After you start the session, try saying one of these:

* I’m hosting a last-minute dinner tonight. I have 30 minutes, two vegetarian friends, one mushroom-hater, and a tiny kitchen. Help me plan a simple menu.
* I’m welcoming guests to a live event in Japan. Say a warm, natural welcome in Japanese — like a host kicking off something special.
* My order number is Orbit-742Q. Repeat it back clearly so I can confirm it’s right.
* Help me practice telling my team we hit our launch milestone. First say it with quiet confidence, then with more excitement.
* I’m planning trivia for a road trip. Give me three trick questions that sound deceivingly simple, then explain each answer in one sentence.

This demo is time-limited. By using it, you agree to OpenAI's [Terms](https://openai.com/index/advancing-voice-intelligence-with-new-models-in-the-api/policies/terms-of-use/) and acknowledge our [Privacy Policy](https://openai.com/index/advancing-voice-intelligence-with-new-models-in-the-api/policies/privacy-policy/).

## 

Voice is becoming one of the most natural ways for people to use software. It lets someone ask for help while driving, change a travel plan while walking through an airport, get support in their preferred language, or move through a task without

# 通过 API 中的新模型推进语音智能

新一代实时语音模型，能够在人们说话时进行推理、翻译和转录。

00:0004:03

收听文章

分享

我们在 API 中推出了三种音频模型，为开发者解锁了一类全新的语音应用。借助这些模型，开发者可以构建更自然、响应更智能且能实时采取行动的语音体验：

* **GPT‑Realtime‑2**，我们首个具备 GPT‑5 级推理能力的语音模型，能够处理更复杂的需求并自然地推进对话。
* **GPT‑Realtime‑Translate**，一种新的实时翻译模型，可将 70 多种输入语言的语音翻译成 13 种输出语言，同时跟上说话者的节奏。
* **GPT‑Realtime‑Whisper**，一种新的流式语音转文本模型，可在说话者讲话时实时转录语音。

# 试用 GPT-Realtime-2

启动会话，然后与 GPT-Realtime-2 自然对话。

启动会话我能问什么？

启动会话后，尝试说出以下其中一项：

* 我今晚要临时举办一场晚宴。我有 30 分钟时间，两位素食朋友，一位不吃蘑菇的客人，还有一个很小的厨房。帮我计划一个简单的菜单。
* 我正在日本迎接一场现场活动的来宾。用日语说一段温暖自然的欢迎词——就像主持人开启一场特别活动那样。
* 我的订单号是 Orbit-742Q。请清晰地复述一遍，以便我确认无误。
* 帮我练习向团队宣布我们达成了发布里程碑。先用沉稳自信的语气说，再用更兴奋的语气说一遍。
* 我正在为一次公路旅行策划 trivia 问答。给我三个看似简单实则狡猾的问题，然后用一句话解释每个答案。

此演示有时间限制。使用即表示您同意 OpenAI 的[条款](https://openai.com/index/advancing-voice-intelligence-with-new-models-in-the-api/policies/terms-of-use/)并确认我们的[隐私政策](https://openai.com/index/advancing-voice-intelligence-with-new-models-in-the-api/policies/privacy-policy/)。

##

items.article_text:
TL;DR: Rolldown, the high-performance Bundler for JavaScript, reached 1.0 stable and production readiness. It features speed, Rollup plugin compatibility, and unique bundler features. Upgrading from the latest RC does not need any code changes.
Rolldown reaches 1.0 [](https://voidzero.dev#rolldown-reaches-1-0)
Rolldown is a Rust-based high-performance JavaScript bundler. It can be used standalone and is powering Vite since its latest major version Vite 8. Now Rolldown itself hits the 1.0 stable milestone.
Rolldown adheres to semantic versioning meaing the ^1.0.0
API is now locked:
- Option names, types, and plugin hook signatures stay backward-compatible.
- No planned breaking changes to Rolldown's public API.
Except for features marked [experimental](https://rolldown.rs/reference/InputOptions.experimental).
Output behavior may still change
Output behavior like Dead Code Elimination, chunking, and inlining heuristics will keep improving, and option defaults may shift to deliver smaller and faster bundles. This does NOT change the runtime behavior of the output. Behavioral changes are noted in release notes.
Built with Vite in mind [](https://voidzero.dev#built-with-vite-in-mind)
One of Rolldown's main goals was to unify Vite's original 2-bundler setup. The previous configuration worked, but it meant two transformation pipelines, two plugin systems, and a growing layer of glue code. It also limited Vite's future capabilities.
Rolldown solved this as a bundler by combining the best parts of existing bundlers. esbuild's speed and Rollup's plugin API. In addition, the bundler provides features neither had. (More on this below)
Swapping Vite's bundler was a 2 year journey. How we got here:
- April 2024: First public
0.10.1
release. - December 2024:
1.0.0-beta.1
, scoping the eventual 1.0 surface area. - May 2025:
, a temporary package, shipped as a technical preview to prepare for the swap and gather feedback from the community.rolldown-vite
- December 2025:
[Vite 8 beta](https://voidzero.dev/posts/announcing-vite-8-beta)shipped with Rolldown as the default bundler. - January 2026:
[Rolldown 1.0 RC](https://voidzero.dev/posts/announcing-rolldown-rc)marked API stability. - March 2026:
[Vite 8 stable](https://vite.dev/blog/announcing-vite8)shipped, making Rolldown the underlying bundler for every Vite user. - May 2026: Rolldown 1.0 stable.
Vite 8 is now Rolldown's main entry point. Meaning new Rolldown features and improvements directly benefit Vite users.
Why switch to Rolldown [](https://voidzero.dev#why-switch-to-rolldown)
Rolldown is a native bundler that combines speed and flexibility through it's plugin API. On top of that, it has unique features that improve the bundling experience and output quality.
Companies like [Framer](https://voidzero.dev/posts/case-study-framer-rolldown) and [PLAID](https://voidzero.dev/posts/case-study-plaid-rolldown) are already using Rolldown in production.
esbuild-like speed [](https://voidzero.dev#esbuild-like-speed)
Rolldown is 10x-30x faster than Rollup (the gap widens as project size grows) and on par with esbuild. Performance is a feature. Rolldown is written in Rust and leverages [Oxc](https://oxc.rs/) to handle the language work like parsing and minification.
Highlights:
- Ramp reduced their build time by 57%
- Mercedes-Benz.io cut their build time down by up to 38%
- Beehiiv reduced their build time by 64%
Full benchmarks: [ rolldown/benchmarks](https://github.com/rolldown/benchmarks).
Rollup plugin-compatibility [](https://voidzero.dev#rollup-plugin-compatibility)
Rolldown needed a Rollup-compatible plugin API to be a drop-in replacement for Vite. Otherwise, developers would have to make massive code changes to migrate to the latest Vite, which is a terrible experience. It also ensures users can continue using their existing plugins. Most Vite plugins work out of the box on Vite 8.
With Rolldown being written in native Rust, there was performance improvement opportunities in the plugin API
items.article_text_zh:
**TL;DR:** Rolldown，高性能 JavaScript 打包器，已发布 1.0 稳定版并达到生产就绪状态。它具备速度优势、Rollup 插件兼容性以及独特的打包器特性。从最新的 RC 版本升级无需任何代码更改。

## Rolldown 达到 1.0 [](https://voidzero.dev#rolldown-reaches-1-0)

Rolldown 是一款基于 Rust 的高性能 JavaScript 打包器。它可以独立使用，并且自其最新主要版本 Vite 8 起，已成为 Vite 的底层引擎。现在，Rolldown 本身达到了 1.0 稳定里程碑。

Rolldown 遵循语义化版本控制，这意味着 `^1.0.0` API 现已锁定：
- 选项名称、类型和插件钩子签名保持向后兼容。
- Rolldown 的公共 API 没有计划中的破坏性更改。
- 标记为 [experimental](https://rolldown.rs/reference/InputOptions.experimental) 的功能除外。

输出行为仍可能发生变化。诸如死代码消除、代码分割和内联启发式算法等输出行为将持续改进，并且选项默认值可能会调整，以生成更小、更快的打包文件。这**不会**改变输出的运行时行为。行为变更将在发布说明中注明。

## 为 Vite 而生 [](https://voidzero.dev#built-with-vite-in-mind)

Rolldown 的主要目标之一是统一 Vite 原有的双打包器架构。之前的配置虽然可行，但意味着两套转换流水线、两套插件系统以及日益增长的胶水代码层。这也限制了 Vite 未来的能力。

Rolldown 作为打包器，通过结合现有打包器的优点解决了这个问题：esbuild 的速度和 Rollup 的插件 API。此外，该打包器还提供了两者都不具备的功能。（更多内容见下文）

替换 Vite 的打包器是一个历时两年的旅程。我们的历程如下：
- **2024 年 4 月**：首次公开发布 `0.10.1` 版本。
- **2024 年 12 月**：发布 `1.0.0-beta.1`，界定了最终 1.0 的功能范围。
- **2025 年 5 月**：临时包 `rolldown-vite` 作为技术预览版发布，为替换做准备并收集社区反馈。
- **2025 年 12 月**：[Vite 8 beta](https://voidzero.dev/posts/announcing-vite-8-beta) 发布，默认使用 Rolldown 作为打包器。
- **2026 年 1 月**：[Rolldown 1.0 RC](https://voidzero.dev/posts/announcing-rolldown-rc) 标志着 API 稳定。
- **2026 年 3 月**：[Vite 8 稳定版](https://vite.dev/blog/announcing-vite8) 发布，使 Rolldown 成为每位 Vite 用户的底层打包器。
- **2026 年 5 月**：Rolldown 1.0 稳定版发布。

Vite 8 现在是 Rolldown 的主要入口点。这意味着新的 Rolldown 功能和改进将直接惠及 Vite 用户。

## 为何切换到 Rolldown [](https://voidzero.dev#why-switch-to-rolldown)

Rolldown 是一个原生打包器，通过其插件 API 结合了速度和灵活性。除此之外，它还拥有独特的功能，可改善打包体验和输出质量。

像 [Framer](https://voidzero.dev/posts/case-study-framer-rolldown) 和 [PLAID](https://voidzero.dev/posts/case-study-plaid-rolldown) 这样的公司已经在生产环境中使用 Rolldown。

### esbuild 级别的速度 [](https://voidzero.dev#esbuild-like-speed)

Rolldown 比 Rollup 快 10 到 30 倍（差距随项目规模增大而扩大），并且与 esbuild 速度相当。性能本身就是一项特性。Rolldown 使用 Rust 编写，并利用 [Oxc](https://oxc.rs/) 处理解析和压缩等语言相关工作。

亮点：
- Ramp 将其构建时间减少了 57%
- Mercedes-Benz.io 将其构建时间缩短了高达 38%
- Beehiiv 将其构建时间减少了 64%

完整基准测试：[rolldown/benchmarks](https://github.com/rolldown/benchmarks)。

### Rollup 插件兼容性 [](https://voidzero.dev#rollup-plugin-compatibility)

Rolldown 需要与 Rollup 兼容的插件 API，才能成为 Vite 的即插即用替代品。否则，开发者将需要进行大量的代码更改才能迁移到最新的 Vite，这将是一种糟糕的体验。这也确保了用户可以继续使用他们现有的插件。大多数 Vite 插件在 Vite 8 上开箱即用。

由于 Rolldown 使用原生 Rust 编写，插件 API 在性能方面也有改进的机会。

items.article_text:
The World Inside Neural Networks
How neural geometry will unlock understanding and control of AI
Authors
Correspondence to atticus@goodfire.ai, ekdeep@goodfire.ai
Published
May 7, 2026
Our world is full of objects and richly structured geometric relationships—the color wheel, road networks, family trees, months of the year—and humans use structured mental concepts to represent and navigate this complex reality[1][Seung, 1999](https://www.pnas.org/doi/10.1073/pnas.93.23.13339), [Behrens et al., 2018](https://www.cell.com/neuron/fulltext/S0896-6273(18)30856-0), [Sohn et al., 2019](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31320220/), [Chung & Abbott, 2021](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0959438821001227?via%3Dihub), [Kriegeskorte & Wei, 2021](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41583-021-00502-3), [Khona & Fiete, 2022](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41583-022-00642-0).
We also know that the inner world of neural networks is full of structure that reflects the structure of the outer world. This is true across models, modalities, and domains: In the internal representations of language models, numbers[2][Nanda et al. 2023](https://arxiv.org/abs/2301.05217), [Zhong et al. 2023](https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.17844), [Zhou et al. 2024](https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2024/hash/2cc8dc30e52798b27d37b795cc153310-Abstract-Conference.html), [Zhou et al. 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.09741), [Kantamneni and Tegmark 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.00873), [Levy and Geva 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.11781), [Fu et al. 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.20817), days of the week, and months of the year[3][Engels et al. 2024](https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.14860), [Modell et al. 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.18235), [Karkada et al. 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.15029), [Prieto et al. 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.09972) are circular loops, and smooth curves represent the years in history[4][Engels et al. 2024](https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.14860), [Modell et al. 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.18235) and characters in a line of text[5][Gurnee et al. 2025](https://transformer-circuits.pub/2025/linebreaks/index.html). In image models, objects' spatial arrangement is recapitulated in activation space[6][Fel et al. 2025a](https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.08638) and colors are represented on smooth surfaces structured by hue, saturation, and lightness[7][Flachot et al. 2022](https://jov.arvojournals.org/article.aspx?articleid=2778712), [Pach et al. 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.12261), [Yeh et al. 2025](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/10297591). In a genomic model, the entire tree of life lies on a complex manifold[8][Pearce et al. 2025](https://www.goodfire.ai/research/phylogeny-manifold), and we discovered a previously-unknown class of Alzheimer's biomarkers as a clean curve in an epigenomic model[9][Wang et al. 2026](https://www.goodfire.ai/research/interpretability-for-alzheimers-detection). At this point, the evidence for curved geometric structure inside neural networks is abundant and undeniable[10][Kozlowski et al. 2018](https://arxiv.org/abs/1803.09288), [Chung et al. 2018](https://journals.aps.org/prx/abstract/10.1103/PhysRevX.8.031003), [Saxe et al. 2019](https://www.pnas.org/doi/abs/10.1073/pnas.1820226116), [Cohen et al. 2020](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-020-14578-5), [Chung and Abbott 2021](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0959438821001227), [Abduo et al. 2021](https://aclanthology.org/2021.conll-1.9/), [Black et al. 2022](https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.12312), [Park et al. 2024](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.00070), [Csordas et al. 2024](https://aclanthology.org/2024.blackboxnlp-1.17.pdf), [Diego-Simon et al. 2024](https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.05571), [Gorton 2024](https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.03662), [Marchetti et al. 2024](https://proceedings.mlr.press/v247/marchetti24a/marchetti24a.pdf), [Bethune et al. 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.18230), [Dooms and Gauderis 2025](https://arx
items.article_text_zh:
# 神经网络内部的世界

神经几何学将如何解锁对AI的理解与控制

**作者**
联系邮箱：atticus@goodfire.ai, ekdeep@goodfire.ai

**发布日期**
2026年5月7日

我们的世界充满了物体和结构丰富的几何关系——色轮、道路网络、家谱、一年中的月份——而人类使用结构化的心智概念来表征和导航这个复杂的现实[1][Seung, 1999](https://www.pnas.org/doi/10.1073/pnas.93.23.13339), [Behrens et al., 2018](https://www.cell.com/neuron/fulltext/S0896-6273(18)30856-0), [Sohn et al., 2019](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31320220/), [Chung & Abbott, 2021](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0959438821001227?via%3Dihub), [Kriegeskorte & Wei, 2021](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41583-021-00502-3), [Khona & Fiete, 2022](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41583-022-00642-0)。

我们也知道，神经网络的内部世界充满了反映外部世界结构的内在结构。这一点在各类模型、模态和领域中都成立：在语言模型的内部表征中，数字[2][Nanda et al. 2023](https://arxiv.org/abs/2301.05217), [Zhong et al. 2023](https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.17844), [Zhou et al. 2024](https://proceedings.neurips.cc/paper_files/paper/2024/hash/2cc8dc30e52798b27d37b795cc153310-Abstract-Conference.html), [Zhou et al. 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.09741), [Kantamneni and Tegmark 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.00873), [Levy and Geva 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.11781), [Fu et al. 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.20817)、星期几和月份[3][Engels et al. 2024](https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.14860), [Modell et al. 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.18235), [Karkada et al. 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.15029), [Prieto et al. 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.09972)呈现为圆形环路，而平滑曲线则表征了历史年份[4][Engels et al. 2024](https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.14860), [Modell et al. 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.18235)和文本行中的字符[5][Gurnee et al. 2025](https://transformer-circuits.pub/2025/linebreaks/index.html)。在图像模型中，物体的空间排列在激活空间中得以复现[6][Fel et al. 2025a](https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.08638)，颜色则被表征在由色相、饱和度和明度结构化的平滑曲面上[7][Flachot et al. 2022](https://jov.arvojournals.org/article.aspx?articleid=2778712), [Pach et al. 2026](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.12261), [Yeh et al. 2025](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/10297591)。在基因组模型中，整个生命之树坐落于一个复杂的流形上[8][Pearce et al. 2025](https://www.goodfire.ai/research/phylogeny-manifold)，而我们发现了一类此前未知的阿尔茨海默病生物标志物，在表观基因组模型中呈现为一条清晰的曲线[9][Wang et al. 2026](https://www.goodfire.ai/research/interpretability-for-alzheimers-detection)。至此，神经网络内部存在弯曲几何结构的证据已丰富且无可辩驳[10][Kozlowski et al. 2018](https://arxiv.org/abs/1803.09288), [Chung et al. 2018](https://journals.aps.org/prx/abstract/10.1103/PhysRevX.8.031003), [Saxe et al. 2019](https://www.pnas.org/doi/abs/10.1073/pnas.1820226116), [Cohen et al. 2020](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-020-14578-5), [Chung and Abbott 2021](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0959438821001227), [Abduo et al. 2021](https://aclanthology.org/2021.conll-1.9/), [Black et al. 2022](https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.12312), [Park et al. 2024](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.00070), [Csordas et al. 2024](https://aclanthology.org/2024.blackboxnlp-1.17.pdf), [Diego-Simon et al. 2024](https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.05571), [Gorton 2024](https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.03662), [Marchetti et al. 2024](https://proceedings.mlr.press/v247/marchetti24a/marchetti24a.pdf), [Bethune et al. 2025](https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.18230), [Dooms and Gauderis 2025](https://arx

Changelog Company handbook Store Work here Trash Zoom Font Share Discord The era of self-driving development is here PostHog Code is the only AI devtool that understands your product, not just your codebase . Identifies product usage patterns Triages bugs and errors Creates pull requests automatically Join the waitlist Test drives begin Spring 2026 The old way to build with AI Coding with AI (cir. 2022-2025) 1. Analyze usage Human 2. Decide what to build Human 3. Prompt & context Human 4. Build Machine 5. Ship Human 1. Analyze usage 2. Decide what to build 3. Prompt & context 4. Build 5. Ship → → → → Human Human Human Machine Human Most AI code editors lack context and wait for you to tell them what to Build ↵ . Coding with AI (cir. 2022-2025) 1. Analyze usage Human 2. Decide what to build Human 3. Prompt & context Human 4. Build Machine 5. Ship Human 1. Analyze usage 2. Decide what to build 3. Prompt & context 4. Build 5. Ship → → → → Human Human Human Machine Human They use your <codebase /> as the source of truth, not how humans (or agents) actually use your product . The PostHog way Signals In-app activity Logs Errors Payments Session recordings Traces Funnel dropoff Feature flags Experiment results Internal threads Support tickets Backlog Transcriptions PostHog Code uses signals from ● production data to diagnose issues and generate pull requests to fix them – before you even know there's a problem. Run it locally or in the cloud – either way, it automatically uses the right AI model for the job. TL;DR: There are plenty of AI coding tools, but only one that knows your product like PostHog Code . Everything you'd expect in an AI coding tool, but way more... Command center Signals inbox Plan Review Skills Manage multiple coding agents in parallel Split screen presets let you monitor agents side-by-side or in a 2x2 or 3x3 grid. Automatic PostHog instrumentation PostHog Code automatically builds new features with the proper PostHog instrumentation so you can progressively roll out changes and monitor their impact. Event instrumentation Tracks new features and changes to existing features as they're built Error tracking Configures exception capture so new code surfaces in PostHog with stack traces Feature flags & rollout conditions Creates the flag, implements isFeatureEnabled , and configures staged rollout conditions Experiments Scaffolds variants, split, and goal metrics Supported LLMs PostHog Code runs on the same models you already use – no markup. OpenAI GPT-5.5 GPT-5.4 Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.6 Claude Opus 4.7 Claude Haiku 4.5 Integrations Linear Slack GitHub Granola Zendesk MCP support Manage MCP servers for your AI agents. Connect external services to extend your agent's capabilities. Attio Manage Attio CRM contacts, companies, and deals. HubSpot Sync contacts, companies, and marketing lifecycle for richer agent context. Stripe Look up customers, subscriptions, and invoices from billing. Salesforce Query accounts, opportunities, and CRM records your agents need in context. Sentry Pull issues, releases, and stack traces so agents debug against real errors. Try it PostHog Code is launching in Spring 2026. Join the waitlist to be the first to try it. Join the waitlist Get updates FAQ What is PostHog Code? What's the difference between PostHog AI and PostHog Code? Why is PostHog building a coding agent? Does it replace Cursor or Claude Code? What models and editors does it work with? What if I don't use PostHog yet? How does it decide what to work on? Where do tasks run – locally or in the cloud? Is my code sent to PostHog? Is my PostHog data safe? Can it modify my PostHog configuration? How much does it cost? Is it open source?
items.article_text_zh:
产品 OS 定价 文档 社区 公司 更多 开始使用 – 免费 1 home.mdx 产品 OS 定价 customers.mdx demo.mov 文档 与真人对话 提问 注册 ↗ 切换到网站模式 为什么选择 PostHog？ 更新日志 公司手册 商店 加入我们 垃圾箱 缩放 字体 分享 Discord

## 自主开发时代已来临

**PostHog Code** 是唯一一款不仅理解你的代码库，更理解你产品的 AI 开发工具。

- 识别产品使用模式
- 排查错误与异常
- 自动创建拉取请求

[加入候补名单] 测试版将于 2026 年春季启动

### 旧式 AI 构建方式

**使用 AI 编码（约 2022-2025 年）**

1. 分析使用情况 → 人类
2. 决定构建内容 → 人类
3. 提示与上下文 → 人类
4. 构建 → 机器
5. 发布 → 人类

大多数 AI 代码编辑器缺乏上下文，只能等待你告诉它们要构建什么。

它们将你的 `<codebase />` 视为唯一真相来源，而非人类（或代理）实际使用你产品的方式。

### PostHog 的方式

**信号**
应用内活动 | 日志 | 错误 | 支付 | 会话录制 | 追踪 | 漏斗流失 | 功能标志 | 实验结果 | 内部线程 | 支持工单 | 积压任务 | 转录

PostHog Code 利用 **生产数据** 中的信号来诊断问题并生成修复它们的拉取请求——甚至在你意识到问题存在之前。你可以在本地或云端运行它——无论哪种方式，它都会自动为任务选择合适的 AI 模型。

简而言之：AI 编码工具有很多，但只有 PostHog Code 像了解你的产品一样了解它。

### 你期望 AI 编码工具应有的一切，甚至更多...

**指挥中心**
信号收件箱 | 计划 | 审查 | 技能

**并行管理多个编码代理**
分屏预设让你可以并排或在 2x2 或 3x3 网格中监控代理。

**自动 PostHog 仪表化**
PostHog Code 在构建新功能时会自动添加正确的 PostHog 仪表化，以便你可以逐步推出变更并监控其影响。

- **事件仪表化**：跟踪新功能及现有功能的变更
- **错误追踪**：配置异常捕获，使新代码在 PostHog 中显示堆栈跟踪
- **功能标志与发布条件**：创建标志，实现 `isFeatureEnabled`，并配置分阶段发布条件
- **实验**：搭建变体、拆分和目标指标

**支持的 LLM**
PostHog Code 运行在你已使用的相同模型上——无需额外标记。
- OpenAI GPT-5.5
- OpenAI GPT-5.4
- Anthropic Claude Sonnet 4.6
- Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7
- Anthropic Claude Haiku 4.5

**集成**
Linear | Slack | GitHub | Granola | Zendesk

**MCP 支持**
为你的 AI 代理管理 MCP 服务器。连接外部服务以扩展代理能力。
- **Attio**：管理 Attio CRM 联系人、公司和交易
- **HubSpot**：同步联系人、公司和营销生命周期，为代理提供更丰富的上下文
- **Stripe**：从计费系统查询客户、订阅和发票
- **Salesforce**：查询代理在上下文中需要的账户、机会和 CRM 记录
- **Sentry**：拉取问题、发布和堆栈跟踪，使代理能针对真实错误进行调试

### 立即体验

PostHog Code 将于 2026 年春季发布。加入候补名单，成为首批体验者。

[加入候补名单] [获取更新]

### 常见问题

- 什么是 PostHog Code？
- PostHog AI 和 PostHog Code 有什么区别？
- 为什么 PostHog 要构建一个编码代理？
- 它会取代 Cursor 或 Claude Code 吗？
- 它支持哪些模型和编辑器？
- 如果我还没使用 PostHog 怎么办？
- 它如何决定要做什么工作？
- 任务在哪里运行——本地还是云端？
- 我的代码会发送到 PostHog 吗？
- 我的 PostHog 数据安全吗？
- 它能修改我的 PostHog 配置吗？
- 费用是多少？
- 它是开源的吗？

# Android Show | I/O 版本

Android 最新动态。观看回顾视频，探索 Android 生态系统的最新更新，包括 Gemini Intelligence、Googlebook、Android Auto 等更多内容。

## Gemini Intelligence

在我们最先进的设备上体验最佳 Gemini。¹ Gemini Intelligence 懂你的节奏，处理繁琐事务，帮你专注于真正重要的事情。Gemini Intelligence 即将在你所在的地方上线——手机、手表、笔记本电脑，还有你的汽车。查看后续动态

## Googlebook

重新定义笔记本电脑品类。认识 Googlebook——以 Gemini 为核心打造的顶级硬件，与你的设备无缝协作。体验 Googlebook

## Android Auto

Android Auto 的下一代。今年晚些时候，Android Auto 将带来惊艳的全新体验、优质娱乐内容，以及更贴心的 Gemini。你的驾驶体验将变得更智能、更有趣。

## 核心体验升级

无论是表达自我，还是与家人朋友保持联系，Android 正在升级你日常使用的方方面面。还有全新方式帮你管理屏幕使用时间。不错过任何精彩。查看开发者剪辑版

了解如何通过全新的用户旅程、主动式体验以及跨创新形态（如 Android XR、Googlebook、Android Auto 等）的自动化开发，为未来构建应用。立即观看

items.article_text: Grok API Company Colossus Careers News Shop SpaceX 𝕏 Try Grok May 06, 2026 New Compute Partnership with Anthropic SpaceXAI has signed an agreement with Anthropic to provide access to Colossus 1. SpaceXAI has signed an agreement with Anthropic to provide access to Colossus 1, one of the world’s largest and fastest-deployed AI supercomputers. Built from the ground up in record time, Colossus delivers unprecedented scale for AI training, fine-tuning, inference, and high-performance computing workloads. Colossus 1 features over 220,000 NVIDIA GPUs, including dense deployments of H100, H200, and next-generation GB200 accelerators. The cluster delivers extreme parallel performance for large language models, multimodal systems, scientific simulations, and generative AI at frontier scale. Anthropic plans to use this additional compute to directly improve capacity for Claude Pro and Claude Max subscribers. As part of this agreement, Anthropic also expressed interest in partnering to develop multiple gigawatts of orbital AI compute capacity. The compute required to train and operate the next generation of these systems is outpacing what terrestrial power, land, and cooling can deliver on the timelines that matter. SpaceX is the only organization with the launch cadence, mass-to-orbit economics, and constellation operations experience to make orbital compute a near-term engineering program rather than a research concept. If engineering challenges can be overcome, space-based compute offers near-limitless sustainable power with less impact on Earth. Try Grok On Web iOS Android Grok on X Products Grok 𝕏 Grok Enterprise Grokipedia API Overview Voice API Imagine API Pricing API Console Login Documentation Company Company Careers Contact News Resources Privacy policy Privacy portal Security Safety Legal Status
items.article_text_zh:
2026年5月6日  
**与Anthropic达成新的算力合作**  
SpaceXAI已与Anthropic签署协议，提供对Colossus 1的访问权限。Colossus 1是全球规模最大、部署速度最快的AI超级计算机之一，从零开始以创纪录的速度建成，为AI训练、微调、推理及高性能计算工作负载提供了前所未有的规模。该集群配备超过22万块NVIDIA GPU，包括密集部署的H100、H200及下一代GB200加速器，可为大语言模型、多模态系统、科学模拟及前沿规模的生成式AI提供极致并行性能。Anthropic计划利用这一新增算力直接提升Claude Pro和Claude Max订阅用户的服务能力。  
作为协议的一部分，Anthropic还表达了合作开发数吉瓦级轨道AI算力容量的意向。训练和运行下一代系统所需的算力，正以远超地球电力、土地和冷却资源在关键时间窗口内所能提供的速度增长。SpaceX是唯一具备发射频率、入轨成本效益及星座运营经验的组织，能将轨道计算从研究概念转化为近期工程计划。若工程挑战得以克服，天基计算将提供近乎无限的可持续能源，同时减少对地球的影响。

items.article_title_extracted: ‎Roll - Fresh film daily App - App Store
items.description:
What if creativity came from less, not more?

With Roll, you get a single film roll every day. You unbox it, and that’s your look for the day. No switching, no tweaking, no second-guessing. Just one perspective to explore.

Each roll is the same for everyone. All over the world, people are capturing their day through the exact same filter. The result is a shared visual language, where the difference isn’t the tool - it’s how you see.

There are no endless options here. No editing tools to hide behind. Just a simple constraint that pushes you to think differently and notice more.

The ordinary starts to feel new again. Light, color, and moments you might have ignored suddenly stand out, simply because you’re looking at them in a new way.

Roll is about slowing down, letting go of perfection, and rediscovering the joy of taking photos.

◼ Break out of filter fatigue
◼ Build a daily creative habit
◼ See how others interpret the same roll
◼ Focus on what matters, not what’s possible

Unbox your roll. Shoot your day. See what happens.
items.description_zh:
如果创意来自“更少”，而非“更多”呢？

有了 Roll，你每天只会收到一卷胶卷。拆开它，这就是你今天的视觉风格。无需切换、无需调整、无需犹豫。只有一个视角，供你探索。

每卷胶卷对所有人都是一样的。在世界各地，人们正通过完全相同的滤镜捕捉他们的一天。结果便是一种共享的视觉语言——差异不在于工具，而在于你如何观察。

这里没有无尽的选择，也没有可供躲藏的编辑工具。只有一个简单的约束，推动你以不同的方式思考，并注意到更多。

平凡的事物开始焕然一新。光线、色彩，以及那些你可能忽略的瞬间，突然变得醒目——仅仅因为你正以一种新的方式看待它们。

Roll 关乎放慢脚步、放下对完美的执念，并重新发现拍照的乐趣。

◼ 摆脱滤镜疲劳
◼ 培养每日创意习惯
◼ 看看他人如何诠释同一卷胶卷
◼ 专注于重要的事，而非可能的事

拆开你的胶卷。拍摄你的一天。看看会发生什么。

items.article_title_extracted: K3s
items.description: K3s is a lightweight, certified Kubernetes distribution designed for IoT and Edge computing, ideal for production workloads in resource-constrained environments. It is easy to install with a compact binary size of under 70MB and supports both ARM64 and ARMv7 architectures, making it suitable for small devices like Raspberry Pi as well as larger servers. K3s simplifies the installation process and enhances security by reducing dependencies. Users can quickly get started by downloading the latest release and following simple commands to set up server and agent nodes. K3s is supported by Rancher and is recognized as a CNCF Sandbox Project.
items.description_zh: K3s 是一款轻量级、经过认证的 Kubernetes 发行版，专为物联网和边缘计算设计，非常适合资源受限环境下的生产工作负载。其安装简便，二进制文件体积小巧（不足70MB），并同时支持ARM64和ARMv7架构，因此既适用于树莓派等小型设备，也能在大型服务器上运行。K3s通过减少依赖项简化了安装流程并增强了安全性。用户只需下载最新版本，按照简单命令即可快速搭建服务器和代理节点。K3s由Rancher提供支持，并已被认可为CNCF沙箱项目。
items.title: K3s
