items.article_text:
How we built a real-time video agent that transforms any single image — photorealistic human, cartoon mascot or fantasy creature — into a fully expressive conversational character at 24fps, with fast end-to-end latency. Zero fine-tuning required.
Runway Characters is a real-time video agent that turns a single reference image into a fully expressive, conversational video character. Built on GWM-1, our General World Model, it produces natural lip-sync, facial expressions and head motions at 24 frames per second (fps) in HD.
Characters has an effective 37 milliseconds of model time per frame (at more than 24 fps), and 1.75 seconds server-side turn-around from when the user stops speaking to when the character starts responding.
This post covers what makes Characters different: the breadth of what it can animate, how we made it real-time and the product ecosystem we built around the model.
The input to Characters is a single reference image. That's it. The system extracts style directly from the image, and immediately begins generating conversational video conditioned on real-time audio.
Below are 720p clips showing Characters animating a single reference image across styles—from photorealistic humans to cartoons, creatures and brand mascots—with natural, expressive conversation.
24fps in real time: Characters runs at an effective 37 milliseconds (ms) of model time per frame and 1.75 seconds server-side turn-around from end-of-speech to first response frame.
Generating HD video at 24fps in an interactive conversational loop is a fundamentally different engineering problem than offline video generation. Offline, you can spend seconds per frame. In a conversation, you have a frame budget of 42 ms (1/24 seconds) – and that's before audio processing, network transit and the perception of “liveness” that collapses if latency creeps up. In our live sessions, we achieve an effective 37 ms of model time per frame, and 1.75 seconds of server-side turn-around (voice agent + video pipeline) from when the user stops speaking to when the character starts responding.
We make this possible with GWM-1's autoregressive frame-by-frame generation: frames are produced sequentially and streamed to the client as they're generated, instead of iteratively denoising an entire clip.
We hit real-time 24fps because of overlap: the diffusion transformer and VAE decoder run concurrently via a pipeline split. That makes the effective iteration cost closer to max(diffusion_time, decode_time) than diffusion_time + decode_time.
Each iteration produces 4 pixel frames, so at 24fps we need one iteration every 167 ms (4/24 seconds). In our measurements, diffusion is 151 ms and VAE decoder is 119 ms; because decode is overlapped, it largely disappears from the critical path. That works out to an effective 37 ms of model time per frame (151 ms / 4 frames), under the 41 ms/frame real-time budget.
Without overlap, diffusion would consume most of the 167 ms budget and decode would push it over. With overlap, decode of frame N−1 runs at the same time as diffusion of frame N.
“Real-time” is also what the user feels end-to-end: the time from when they stop speaking to when the character starts responding. Putting it together: 1185 ms (voice agent) + 567 ms (video pipeline) = 1.75 seconds server-side latency in this session. Client↔server network adds 200 ms on each side (400 ms round trip), depending on connection quality.
The biggest lever to bring this down further is the voice agent: the video pipeline is already fast, and we expect the overall turn-around to drop as we improve voice agent response time.
Below are four of the key model- and systems-level optimizations that make this real-time performance possible:
We shard inference across devices, where the main constraint is communication overhead. We pipeline tensor‑parallel diffusion with VAE decoder on a dedicated device so decode doesn’t contend with the transformer.
Autoregressive video grows the KV cache every fra
items.article_text_zh:
## 我们如何构建一个实时视频智能体，能将任意单张图片——逼真人类、卡通吉祥物或奇幻生物——转化为以24fps运行的、富有表现力的对话角色。无需微调。

Runway Characters 是一个实时视频智能体，能将单张参考图片转化为一个富有表现力、可对话的视频角色。它基于我们的通用世界模型 GWM-1，能以每秒24帧（fps）的高清画质生成自然的唇形同步、面部表情和头部运动。

Characters 每帧的有效模型处理时间为37毫秒（超过24fps），从用户停止说话到角色开始响应的服务器端周转时间为1.75秒。

本文将介绍 Characters 的独特之处：其可驱动内容的广度、我们实现实时性的方法，以及围绕该模型构建的产品生态系统。

Characters 的输入仅是一张参考图片。仅此而已。系统直接从图片中提取风格，并立即开始生成基于实时音频条件化的对话视频。

以下是720p的片段，展示了 Characters 如何驱动单张参考图片跨越不同风格——从逼真人类到卡通、生物和品牌吉祥物——并实现自然、富有表现力的对话。

**24fps 实时运行**：Characters 每帧的有效模型处理时间为37毫秒，从语音结束到生成首帧响应画面的服务器端周转时间为1.75秒。

在交互式对话循环中以24fps生成高清视频，与离线视频生成是截然不同的工程难题。离线时，每帧可以花费数秒。而在对话中，每帧的预算只有42毫秒（1/24秒）——这还没算上音频处理、网络传输以及一旦延迟增加就会破坏的“实时感”。在我们的实时会话中，我们实现了每帧37毫秒的有效模型处理时间，以及从用户停止说话到角色开始响应的1.75秒服务器端周转时间（语音智能体 + 视频管线）。

我们通过 GWM-1 的自回归逐帧生成技术实现了这一点：帧是顺序生成的，并在生成的同时流式传输到客户端，而不是迭代地对整个片段进行去噪。

我们之所以能达到实时24fps，是因为采用了重叠处理：扩散变换器和VAE解码器通过管线拆分并发运行。这使得有效迭代成本更接近于 max(扩散时间, 解码时间)，而非 扩散时间 + 解码时间。

每次迭代生成4个像素帧，因此在24fps下，每167毫秒（4/24秒）需要一次迭代。在我们的测量中，扩散耗时151毫秒，VAE解码器耗时119毫秒；由于解码是重叠的，它基本上从关键路径上消失了。这相当于每帧37毫秒的有效模型处理时间（151毫秒 / 4帧），低于41毫秒/帧的实时预算。

如果没有重叠，扩散将消耗167毫秒预算中的大部分，而解码会使其超限。有了重叠，第N-1帧的解码与第N帧的扩散同时运行。

“实时”也指用户端到端的感受：从他们停止说话到角色开始响应的时间。综合来看：1185毫秒（语音智能体）+ 567毫秒（视频管线）= 本次会话中1.75秒的服务器端延迟。客户端↔服务器网络根据连接质量，每侧增加200毫秒（往返400毫秒）。

进一步降低延迟的最大杠杆在于语音智能体：视频管线已经很快，我们预计随着语音智能体响应时间的改善，整体周转时间会进一步下降。

以下是实现这种实时性能的四个关键模型和系统级优化：

我们将推理任务分片到多个设备上，主要限制是通信开销。我们将张量并行扩散与VAE解码器通过管线方式部署在专用设备上，这样解码就不会与变换器争抢资源。

自回归视频会随着每帧生成而增长KV缓存。

items.article_text: Skip to main content Product Why Fathom Pricing Resources The Google Analytics Alternative Why you should ditch Google Analytics No cookies & GDPR compliant Actionable insights while staying compliant with privacy laws About us 100% independently owned, zero VC pressure Help Center & Docs API Blog Changelog Status Free trial Login Pricing Free trial Log in Menu Fathom acquires Gauges news Jack Ellis · Apr 20, 2026 We are so excited to announce that Fathom has acquired Gauges , the beautiful, real-time, simple analytics product that paved the way for companies like ours. A brief history Gauges was founded in 2011 by Ordered List, who looked at the website analytics landscape and thought, “Why can’t analytics be simple?” Their small but mighty team shipped an incredible product, which took off immediately. Nine months later, Ordered List (and Gauges) was acquired by GitHub. In 2017, GitHub sold Gauges to ReportGarden Inc, who had big plans for Gauges and kept it running for the next nine years. Then, in March 2026, ReportGarden made the hard decision to announce the sunset of Gauges. It was no longer feasible for them to maintain it whilst also actively building their main product. We immediately had Gauges customers emailing us about importing their data into Fathom, and I knew we had to do something, so I emailed their CEO. And then, on 15th April, we acquired Gauges. What happens now Fathom offers everything Gauges did and more, and we will be migrating all of their customers over to our platform whilst preserving their historical data. We’ll also soon be adding Air Traffic Live, popular with Gauges customers, to Fathom as a first-class feature. Thank you To the Ordered List team, thank you for building Gauges and for the beautifully simple codebase and data structure. To ReportGarden Inc, thank you for the smooth process of getting to a deal that worked for both of us. ReportGarden is now doubling down on their reporting hub and insights hub. You can learn more about them here . Return to the Fathom Analytics blog BIO Jack Ellis, CTO Recent blog posts Fathom acquires Gauges Announcing: Fathom V4 Fathom Analytics has been acquired I made a mistake Reducing our AWS bill by $100,000 Tired of how time consuming and complex Google Analytics can be? Try Fathom Analytics: Start a free trial Sign up for our monthly newsletter via email, or grab the RSS feed . items.article_text_zh:
# Fathom 收购 Gauges

Jack Ellis · 2026年4月20日

我们非常激动地宣布，Fathom 已收购 **Gauges**——这款美观、实时、简洁的统计分析产品为像我们这样的公司铺平了道路。

## 简史

Gauges 由 Ordered List 于 2011 年创立，他们审视网站分析领域时不禁思考："为什么分析工具不能简单一点？"他们虽小但强大的团队打造了一款令人难以置信的产品，并迅速走红。九个月后，Ordered List（及其 Gauges）被 GitHub 收购。

2017 年，GitHub 将 Gauges 出售给 ReportGarden Inc，后者对 Gauges 寄予厚望，并持续运营了九年。随后，在 2026 年 3 月，ReportGarden 做出了艰难的决定，宣布 Gauges 将停止服务。在积极开发其主力产品的同时，他们已无法再继续维护 Gauges。

我们立即收到了 Gauges 客户的邮件，询问如何将数据导入 Fathom，我知道我们必须做点什么，于是给他们的 CEO 发了邮件。然后，在 4 月 15 日，我们收购了 Gauges。

## 接下来会发生什么

Fathom 提供了 Gauges 所具备的一切功能，甚至更多。我们将把所有 Gauges 客户迁移到我们的平台，同时保留他们的历史数据。我们还将很快把 Gauges 用户喜爱的 Air Traffic Live 作为一级功能添加到 Fathom 中。

## 致谢

感谢 Ordered List 团队，感谢你们打造了 Gauges，以及那优美简洁的代码库和数据结构。感谢 ReportGarden Inc，感谢你们促成了这次对双方都有利的顺利交易。ReportGarden 现在将全力投入其报告中心和洞察中心。您可以[在此处](https://reportgarden.com)了解更多关于他们的信息。

[返回 Fathom Analytics 博客](https://usefathom.com/blog)

**BIO**  
Jack Ellis，CTO

items.article_title_extracted: ‎Things take time — Logbook App - App Store
items.description:
Good things take time. Real progress is built through small things done consistently — it won’t feel like much, until it does.

Things take time is an app for tracking what matters, the small things, and seeing how they compound over time.


KEY FEATURES

• Track anything
From habits and routines to personal metrics, notes, and daily actions — build trackers that fit your life, not the other way around.

• See everything side by side
Most apps show one tracker at a time, which hides the bigger picture. This one shows them all together so you can see patterns across your month.

• Your month at a glance
See how small things add up across your month, so you can stay consistent and notice real progress.

• Built for ease of use
Tracking should feel effortless. Everything is designed to be fast, simple, and intuitive.

• Privacy first
Your data stays on your device or in your iCloud. Nothing is collected, and nothing is tracked.


Progress is often invisible in the moment. Things take time was built to make it visible — by showing how small things add up over time.


Terms & Privacy: https://github.com/chesterhow/things-take-time/blob/main/terms-privacy.md
items.description_zh:
好事多磨。真正的进步源于日复一日坚持做小事——起初或许微不足道，终将厚积薄发。

《好事多磨》是一款追踪重要之事、记录点滴日常的应用，让你亲眼见证微小积累如何随时间复利增长。

核心功能

• 万物皆可追踪
从习惯养成、日常作息到个人数据、笔记感悟、每日行动——打造契合你生活的追踪器，而非让生活迁就工具。

• 全局一览无余
多数应用一次只展示一个追踪项，反而掩盖了全局图景。本应用将所有数据并列呈现，让你清晰洞察整月的行为模式。

• 月度全景视图
直观查看每日小事如何汇聚成月度轨迹，助你保持节奏，见证真实进步。

• 极致便捷体验
追踪本该毫不费力。所有设计皆以快速、简洁、直观为宗旨。

• 隐私至上
你的数据仅存于设备或iCloud中。绝不收集任何信息，不做任何追踪。


进步往往在当下难以察觉。《好事多磨》正是为让进步可视化而生——通过展现小事如何随时间累积，让成长轨迹清晰可见。

条款与隐私：https://github.com/chesterhow/things-take-time/blob/main/terms-privacy.md

items.article_text: THREE.JS ROADMAP Assets Courses Blog FAQ Login Sign Up Asset Three.js Sky Pro A complete, real-time sky system for Three.js. Volumetric clouds, dynamic atmosphere, and physically-inspired lighting. Coming Soon — Join the waitlist Join Waitlist On this page Features Features Three.js Sky Pro is a production-ready sky rendering engine built on Three.js WebGPU. It gives you a complete, dynamic sky out of the box — volumetric clouds, atmospheric scattering, sun lighting, and tone mapping — all rendered in real time with no baked textures or static skyboxes. Use the included demo to tune the sky to your scene. Adjust cloud coverage, density, and shape until it looks right, then export your settings directly into your project. Drag the sun across the sky and the whole atmosphere shifts — clouds pick up warm light at the edges, glow from within near sunset, and cast nsoft shadows through the layer. Add wind and everything drifts naturally. Designed to run on all devices, from mobile to high-refresh desktop displays. What You Get Complete drop-in sky system for Three.js projects Volumetric 3D clouds with full creative control over shape, density, and detail Dynamic atmosphere with configurable horizon, zenith, and sun colors Physically-inspired cloud lighting with scattering, extinction, and inner glow Wind-driven animation and real-time time-of-day changes Tunable quality settings for any performance budget WebGPU-powered with Three.js Shading Language (TSL) Links Home Assets Courses FAQ Legal Privacy Policy Cookie Policy Terms of Service Contact support@threejsroadmap.com X LinkedIn Instagram YouTube GitHub © 2026 Three.js Roadmap. All rights reserved. Privacy Settings
items.article_text_zh:
THREE.JS ROADMAP 资源 课程 博客 常见问题 登录 注册

## 资源 Three.js Sky Pro

一个完整、实时的 Three.js 天空系统。包含体积云、动态大气和基于物理的照明。即将推出——加入等候名单

[加入等候名单]

---

### 本页内容

- 功能特性

---

### 功能特性

Three.js Sky Pro 是一款基于 Three.js WebGPU 构建的生产级天空渲染引擎。它为您提供开箱即用的完整动态天空——体积云、大气散射、太阳光照和色调映射——全部实时渲染，无需烘焙纹理或静态天空盒。

使用附带的演示工具，将天空调整至与您的场景匹配。调整云层覆盖、密度和形状，直到效果满意，然后将设置直接导出到您的项目中。将太阳拖过天空，整个大气层随之变化——云层边缘捕捉暖光，在日落附近从内部发光，并通过云层投射柔和阴影。添加风效后，一切都会自然飘动。

专为在所有设备上运行而设计，从移动端到高刷新率桌面显示器。

#### 您将获得

- 适用于 Three.js 项目的完整即插即用天空系统
- 体积3D云，可完全创意控制形状、密度和细节
- 动态大气，可配置地平线、天顶和太阳颜色
- 基于物理的云光照，包含散射、消光和内部发光
- 风驱动动画和实时时间变化
- 可调节的质量设置，适应任何性能预算
- 基于 WebGPU 和 Three.js 着色语言 (TSL)

---

items.article_title_extracted: Copy Fail — CVE-2026-31431
items.description: Copy Fail (CVE-2026-31431): a 732-byte Linux LPE — straight-line, no race, no per-distro offsets. Same Python script roots Ubuntu, Amazon Linux, RHEL, SUSE since 2017. Page-cache write bypasses on-disk file-integrity tools and crosses container boundaries. Found by Xint Code.
items.description_zh: Copy Fail (CVE-2026-31431)：一个 732 字节的 Linux 本地提权漏洞——直线攻击，无竞态条件，无需跨发行版偏移。同一 Python 脚本自 2017 年起即可在 Ubuntu、Amazon Linux、RHEL、SUSE 上获取 root 权限。页面缓存写入绕过磁盘文件完整性检查工具，并可跨越容器边界。由 Xint Code 发现。
items.title: Copy Fail — CVE-2026-31431

items.article_text:
Response Caching: Zero Cost for Identical Requests
Brian Thomas ·
You can now add X-OpenRouter-Cache: true
to your chat completions, responses, messages, or embeddings requests to start caching identical calls. The first call hits the provider and gets billed normally. Every identical call after that returns the same response in a tiny fraction of the time, with zero tokens billed.
[View the response caching docs(opens in new tab)](https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/features/response-caching)
What it does
Response caching sits in front of the model provider. When you send a request with caching enabled, OpenRouter hashes the request body, model, API key, and streaming mode into a cache key. If an identical request was made before and hasn't expired, the cached response comes back immediately. No provider call, no token consumption, no charge.
Both streaming and non-streaming requests work. Cached streaming responses replay through the same pipeline, so your client code doesn't need to change. Text, images, audio, documents, and tool calls all cache normally. Multimodal inputs (base64 images, audio clips, file attachments) are included in the cache key hash. One caveat: very large multimodal payloads that get offloaded internally for processing aren't eligible for caching. Standard-sized requests cache fine.
Response caching is separate from prompt caching. Prompt caching (which many providers offer natively) reduces the cost of the prompt portion when messages share a common prefix. Response caching skips the provider entirely and returns the full response from OpenRouter's edge cache.
Reduces response times from seconds to milliseconds
Cached responses come back in 80-300ms, most of which is serialization and network. The cache lookup itself averages 4ms. For comparison, a typical uncached request to Gemini 2.5 Flash takes about 1.3 seconds, Kimi K2.6 takes 4.6 seconds, and GPT-5.5 takes 9.1 seconds. Cache hits are billed at zero: no prompt tokens, no completion tokens, no charge.
Enable it with a request header or with presets
Add the X-OpenRouter-Cache: true
header to each API call you want to be eligible:
Presets. Enable caching for all requests using a specific preset by setting cache_enabled: true
[in the preset config(opens in new tab)](https://openrouter.ai/workspaces/default/presets). No header needed on individual requests.
You can control how long responses stay cached with X-OpenRouter-Cache-TTL
(1 second to 24 hours, default 5 minutes). Need a fresh response? Send X-OpenRouter-Cache-Clear: true
to bust the cache for that specific request.
Response headers tell you what happened: X-OpenRouter-Cache-Status: HIT
or MISS
, plus X-OpenRouter-Cache-Age
and X-OpenRouter-Cache-TTL
so you can see exactly how the cache is performing.
Where it helps most
Agent retries. When an agent workflow fails partway through, you can retry from the top. Cached steps return instantly and for free, so you only pay for the new work.
Test suites. Run your LLM-backed tests repeatedly without burning tokens. After the first run populates the cache, subsequent runs are deterministic and free.
Repeated context processing. If your app sends the same prompt to the same model (same system prompt, same user input, same parameters), only the first call costs anything.
Available now across most generation endpoints
The cache is scoped to your API key. Different keys (even under the same account) don't share cache entries.
The feature works across /chat/completions
, /responses
, /messages
, and /embeddings
. Other endpoints — legacy /completions
, /audio/speech
(TTS), /audio/transcriptions
(STT), /rerank
, and video generation — are not yet supported. It's currently in beta, and we're watching how it performs before locking down the API surface.
Cache hits don't count toward provider rate limits (since the request never reaches the provider), and they're visible in your [Activity log(opens in new tab)](https://openrouter.ai/activity) with a cache indi
items.article_text_zh:
缓存：相同请求零成本
Brian Thomas ·

现在，您可以在聊天补全、响应、消息或嵌入请求中添加 `X-OpenRouter-Cache: true`，以开始缓存相同的调用。首次调用会访问提供商并按正常方式计费。此后每次相同的调用都会在极短的时间内返回相同的响应，且不产生任何令牌费用。
[查看响应缓存文档（在新标签页中打开）](https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/features/response-caching)

**它的作用**

响应缓存位于模型提供商之前。当您发送启用了缓存的请求时，OpenRouter 会将请求体、模型、API 密钥和流式模式哈希成一个缓存键。如果之前有过相同的请求且尚未过期，缓存的响应会立即返回。无需调用提供商，不消耗令牌，不产生费用。

流式和非流式请求均可工作。缓存的流式响应会通过相同的管道重放，因此您的客户端代码无需更改。文本、图像、音频、文档和工具调用均可正常缓存。多模态输入（base64 图像、音频片段、文件附件）会包含在缓存键哈希中。一个注意事项：因内部处理而被卸载的非常大的多模态负载不符合缓存条件。标准大小的请求可以正常缓存。

响应缓存与提示缓存是分开的。提示缓存（许多提供商原生支持）在消息共享公共前缀时，会降低提示部分的成本。响应缓存则完全跳过提供商，直接从 OpenRouter 的边缘缓存返回完整响应。

**将响应时间从秒级降至毫秒级**

缓存的响应在 80-300 毫秒内返回，其中大部分时间是序列化和网络传输。缓存查找本身平均只需 4 毫秒。作为对比，一个典型的未缓存请求到 Gemini 2.5 Flash 大约需要 1.3 秒，Kimi K2.6 需要 4.6 秒，GPT-5.5 需要 9.1 秒。缓存命中不计费：无提示令牌、无补全令牌、无任何费用。

**通过请求头或预设启用**

将 `X-OpenRouter-Cache: true` 头添加到您希望符合条件的每个 API 调用中：

预设。通过在[预设配置（在新标签页中打开）](https://openrouter.ai/workspaces/default/presets)中设置 `cache_enabled: true`，为使用特定预设的所有请求启用缓存。单个请求无需添加头。

您可以使用 `X-OpenRouter-Cache-TTL` 控制响应在缓存中的保留时间（1 秒到 24 小时，默认 5 分钟）。需要新的响应？发送 `X-OpenRouter-Cache-Clear: true` 来清除该特定请求的缓存。

响应头会告诉您发生了什么：`X-OpenRouter-Cache-Status: HIT` 或 `MISS`，以及 `X-OpenRouter-Cache-Age` 和 `X-OpenRouter-Cache-TTL`，以便您准确了解缓存的运行情况。

**最适用的场景**

*   **Agent 重试。** 当 agent 工作流中途失败时，您可以从头重试。缓存的步骤会立即且免费返回，因此您只需为新的工作付费。
*   **测试套件。** 反复运行您的 LLM 支持的测试，而无需消耗令牌。首次运行填充缓存后，后续运行将是确定性的且免费的。
*   **重复上下文处理。** 如果您的应用向同一模型发送相同的提示（相同的系统提示、相同的用户输入、相同的参数），则只有第一次调用会产生费用。

**现已可在大多数生成端点使用**

缓存范围限定在您的 API 密钥内。不同的密钥（即使在同一账户下）不会共享缓存条目。

该功能适用于 `/chat/completions`、`/responses`、`/messages` 和 `/embeddings` 端点。其他端点——遗留的 `/completions`、`/audio/speech`（TTS）、`/audio/transcriptions`（STT）、`/rerank` 和视频生成——尚不支持。目前处于测试阶段，我们正在观察其性能，然后再锁定 API 接口。

缓存命中不计入提供商速率限制（因为请求从未到达提供商），并且会在您的[活动日志（在新标签页中打开）](https://openrouter.ai/activity)中显示，并带有缓存指示器。

items.article_title_extracted: 🦀 Crabbox Docs - Crabbox Docs
items.description:
Crabbox is a shared remote testbox system designed for OpenClaw maintainers, allowing developers to keep their local workflows while offloading compute tasks to cloud resources. Key features include:

1. **Local Loop, Remote Box**: Developers sync their local files to a rented Linux machine for remote execution.
2. **Credential Management**: A Cloudflare Worker manages provider credentials, while the local CLI only needs a bearer token.
3. **Cost Management**: Operates with TTL-limited machines, monthly spending caps, and usage tracking on a per-user basis.
4. **Warm Boxes**: `crabbox warmup` allows users to maintain a ready state for subsequent runs, improving efficiency.
5. **Server Flexibility**: Can utilize Hetzner or AWS Spot instances when server capacity is constrained.
6. **Integration with Actions**: Reuses GitHub Actions in local runs.

### How It Works:
- The CLI connects to a Cloudflare Worker to provision a cloud instance, sync files, execute commands, and manage resource releases.

### Installation & Quick Start:
- Install via Homebrew, log in to configure, and execute commands using `crabbox run` and `crabbox warmup`.

### Additional Tools:
- Includes an OpenClaw plugin for integrating Crabbox operations.

### Documentation:
- Various resources available to help understand how to use and operate Crabbox.
items.description_zh:
Crabbox 是一个专为 OpenClaw 维护者设计的共享远程测试盒系统，允许开发者保持本地工作流程的同时，将计算任务卸载到云端资源。主要特性包括：

1. **本地循环，远程盒子**：开发者将本地文件同步到租用的 Linux 机器上进行远程执行。
2. **凭证管理**：Cloudflare Worker 管理提供商凭证，本地 CLI 仅需持有 bearer 令牌。
3. **成本管理**：通过 TTL 限制的机器、月度支出上限和基于用户的使用跟踪来运作。
4. **预热盒子**：`crabbox warmup` 允许用户为后续运行维持就绪状态，提升效率。
5. **服务器灵活性**：在服务器容量受限时，可使用 Hetzner 或 AWS Spot 实例。
6. **与 Actions 集成**：在本地运行中复用 GitHub Actions。

### 工作原理：
- CLI 连接到 Cloudflare Worker 以配置云实例、同步文件、执行命令并管理资源释放。

### 安装与快速入门：
- 通过 Homebrew 安装，登录配置后，使用 `crabbox run` 和 `crabbox warmup` 执行命令。

### 附加工具：
- 包含用于集成 Crabbox 操作的 OpenClaw 插件。

### 文档：
- 提供多种资源，帮助理解如何使用和操作 Crabbox。
items.title: 🦀 Crabbox Docs - Crabbox Docs
items.title_zh: # 🦀 Crabbox 文档 - Crabbox 文档

items.article_text: Products Community Learn Pricing Downloads Rive Blog Free Rive AI Agent Starting today, the Rive Agent is available to everyone, including Free users. AI should make it easier for anyone to learn and build in Rive, regardless of plan. by Guido Rosso - Thursday, April 30, 2026 What just landed The #1 thing we heard: the Agent eats credits too fast. So we introduced more efficient models that cost significantly less, and added an Ask mode for questions that don't need the full Agent. This is very new and we're releasing it early to get your feedback. Here's how AI works across plans: Free — Agent for everyone. It has a capacity, you use it with each prompt, and it recharges hourly. Cadet — Same free Agent modes. Cadet's purpose is to be the most affordable way to export Rive files, not a premium AI plan. At $9/mo, we can't offer a compelling premium AI experience (most paid AI plans start at $20/mo for a reason). We'd rather give you free modes that work well than premium access that runs out fast. New Cadet plans don't include AI credits. If you were already on Cadet before this change, you keep your $5/mo and access to the premium plans. That's grandfathered. Voyager — Premium models, $20/mo in recurring credits that refresh monthly, plus the ability to top up. What's still coming We're working on a bunch of optimizations based on your feedback, which will be landing soon. The Agent will be smarter about context, sending only what it needs, and picking tools more selectively. We're also exploring different ways to let you connect your external agents to the Rive Editor (we've heard your requests to keep working on MCP and the ability to bring your own API keys). This is early We're watching closely and shipping fast. Come tell us what's working (and what isn't) in the Rive Community or Discord . Start building production-ready graphics Get Started Join our newsletter Get all the latest Rive news delivered to your inbox. items.article_text_zh:
# Rive AI Agent 现已免费开放

从今天起，Rive Agent 面向所有人开放，包括免费用户。AI 应该让每个人都能更轻松地学习和使用 Rive 进行创作，无论你选择哪个方案。

作者：Guido Rosso - 2026年4月30日，星期四

## 最新动态

我们听到最多的反馈是：Agent 消耗积分太快了。因此，我们引入了成本显著降低的更高效模型，并新增了"提问模式"，用于不需要完整 Agent 功能的简单问题。这还处于非常早期的阶段，我们提前发布是为了收集大家的反馈。

以下是 AI 在各方案中的工作方式：

- **Free（免费版）** — 面向所有人的 Agent。它有一定的容量限制，每次提问都会消耗，并且每小时自动补充。
- **Cadet（学员版）** — 与免费版相同的 Agent 模式。Cadet 的定位是导出 Rive 文件最经济的方式，而非高级 AI 方案。每月 9 美元的价格，我们无法提供有吸引力的高级 AI 体验（大多数付费 AI 方案从每月 20 美元起步是有原因的）。我们更愿意提供运行良好的免费模式，而不是消耗很快的高级访问权限。新的 Cadet 方案不包含 AI 积分。如果你在此次变更前已经是 Cadet 用户，则保留每月 5 美元的价格和高级方案访问权限。这是老用户特权。
- **Voyager（航海家版）** — 高级模型，每月 20 美元，包含每月重置的循环积分，并支持额外充值。

## 即将推出的功能

我们正在根据大家的反馈进行一系列优化，很快就会上线。Agent 将更智能地处理上下文，只发送必要信息，并更精准地选择工具。我们也在探索不同的方式，让你可以将外部 Agent 连接到 Rive 编辑器（我们已收到大家关于继续支持 MCP 和自带 API 密钥的请求）。

## 这还只是开始

我们正在密切关注并快速迭代。欢迎来 Rive Community 或 Discord 告诉我们哪些功能好用（以及哪些不好用）。

## 开始构建可用于生产环境的图形

立即开始

## 订阅我们的新闻通讯

将所有最新的 Rive 新闻直接发送到你的收件箱。

AI doesn’t rank pages. It picks answers. AI doesn’t rank pages. It picks answers. AI doesn’t rank pages. It picks answers. Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) is the practice of making your content easy for AI systems like ChatGPT, Perplexity, and Gemini to find, understand, and quote. As more people get answers directly from AI instead of clicking search results, being invisible to AI means being invisible to your audience. AI answers are replacing search clicks Over 60% of searches now end without a click. If AI doesn’t surface your site, users may never find you at all. Structure and trust signals matter more than ever AI models favor pages with clear metadata, structured content, and authoritative sources. Bad structure means your content gets skipped entirely. Most websites aren’t ready — yours can be The vast majority of sites still lack the basic signals AI needs. A few targeted fixes can move you from invisible to frequently cited. 扫描你的网站

此评估扫描一个 URL 并使用 AI——结果可能不完美。请将其作为参考，而非最终答案。评分反映我们自己的方法论，可能与其他 AEO 报告不同。

AI 不排名页面。它选择答案。AI 不排名页面。它选择答案。AI 不排名页面。它选择答案。

答案引擎优化（AEO）是一种让你的内容易于被 ChatGPT、Perplexity 和 Gemini 等 AI 系统找到、理解和引用的实践。随着越来越多的人直接从 AI 获取答案，而不是点击搜索结果，对 AI 不可见就意味着对你的受众不可见。

AI 答案正在取代搜索点击
超过 60% 的搜索现在以无点击结束。如果 AI 不展示你的网站，用户可能永远找不到你。

结构和信任信号比以往更重要
AI 模型偏爱具有清晰元数据、结构化内容和权威来源的页面。糟糕的结构意味着你的内容会被完全跳过。

大多数网站尚未准备好——你的可以
绝大多数网站仍然缺乏 AI 所需的基本信号。几个有针对性的修复就能让你从不可见变为频繁被引用。

items.article_text:
Astro 6.2 is here! This release brings an experimental custom logger with built-in JSON output, perfect for structured logging and coding agents. It also includes a new SVG optimizer API, a font file URL helper, and more.
Explore what’s new in this release:
[SVG optimizer](https://astro.build#svg-optimizer)[Experimental Logger](https://astro.build#experimental-logger)[Experimental:](https://astro.build#experimental-getfontfileurl)getFontFileURL()
[Astro v7 alpha](https://astro.build#astro-v7-alpha)allowedHosts
for preview servers"jsx"
option forcompressHTML
To upgrade an existing project, use the automated @astrojs/upgrade
CLI tool. Alternatively, upgrade manually by running the upgrade command for your package manager:
SVG optimizer
Astro’s experimental SVG optimization has been redesigned with a new svgOptimizer
option that replaces the previous svgo
flag. Instead of being tied directly to SVGO, the new API introduces an SvgOptimizer
interface that any optimization library can implement. Astro ships with a built-in svgoOptimizer()
based on [SVGO](https://svgo.dev/), but you can now swap in alternatives like [OxVG](https://github.com/noahbald/oxvg).
When enabled, all imported SVG files used as components are optimized at build time for smaller file sizes and better performance. You can pass a [SVGO configuration object](https://svgo.dev/) to customize the optimization:
If you were previously using experimental.svgo
, update your config to use the new svgOptimizer
option instead.
For more information, see the [experimental SVG optimization docs](https://docs.astro.build/en/reference/experimental-flags/svg-optimization/).
Experimental Logger
It’s now possible to control the Astro logger with custom loggers. As part of the experimental feature, Astro ships with built-in loggers, such as a JSON logger. If you use coding agents to work with Astro, structured logs will definitely help your workflow.
JSON logger
You can enable JSON logging via configuration using experimental.logger
, or via CLI using the flag --experimentalJson
:
Custom logger
You can also create your own custom logger by implementing the correct interface:
For more information on enabling and using this feature in your project, see the [Experimental Logger docs](https://docs.astro.build/en/reference/experimental-flags/logger/).
For a complete overview and to give feedback on this experimental API, see the [Custom logger RFC](https://github.com/withastro/roadmap/blob/logger/proposals/0059-custom-logger.md).
Experimental: getFontFileURL()
You can now load font file data during prerendering using the new experimental_getFontFileURL()
helper exported from astro:assets
. This makes it possible to use Astro-managed fonts with tools like [Satori](https://github.com/vercel/satori) for generating Open Graph images at build time, something that previously required workarounds with undocumented internal paths.
For more information, see the [Fonts API documentation](https://docs.astro.build/en/guides/fonts/#accessing-font-data-programmatically).
Astro v7 alpha
Work on the next major version of Astro has begun. The first Astro 7 alpha includes two big changes:
Vite 8 support
Astro 7 upgrades to [Vite 8](https://vite.dev/blog/announcing-vite8), the latest major version of the build tool that powers Astro. This is a breaking change for Astro integrations and plugins that depend on Vite internals, but most Astro users should be able to upgrade without any changes to their project code.
Stable Rust compiler
The Rust-based Astro compiler, previously available as an experimental flag (experimental.rustCompiler
), is now the default and only compiler in Astro 7. The Rust compiler delivers significantly faster build times compared to the previous Go-based compiler. The experimental.rustCompiler
flag is no longer needed and can be removed from your config.
Try it out
The Astro 7 alpha is available for early testing:
We’d love to hear your feedback! Please report any issues you encounte
items.article_text_zh:
Astro 6.2 来了！本次发布带来了实验性的自定义日志记录器，内置 JSON 输出，非常适合结构化日志记录和编码代理。此外，还包含一个新的 SVG 优化器 API、一个字体文件 URL 辅助工具等。

探索本次发布的新内容：
[SVG 优化器](https://astro.build#svg-optimizer)[实验性日志记录器](https://astro.build#experimental-logger)[实验性：](https://astro.build#experimental-getfontfileurl)getFontFileURL()
[Astro v7 alpha](https://astro.build#astro-v7-alpha)预览服务器的allowedHosts
compressHTML的"jsx"选项
要升级现有项目，请使用自动化工具 @astrojs/upgrade CLI。或者，通过运行包管理器的升级命令手动升级：

SVG 优化器

Astro 的实验性 SVG 优化已重新设计，新增了 svgOptimizer 选项，取代了之前的 svgo 标志。新的 API 不再直接绑定到 SVGO，而是引入了一个 SvgOptimizer 接口，任何优化库都可以实现该接口。Astro 内置了一个基于 [SVGO](https://svgo.dev/) 的 svgoOptimizer()，但现在您可以替换为 [OxVG](https://github.com/noahbald/oxvg) 等替代方案。

启用后，所有作为组件导入的 SVG 文件将在构建时进行优化，以获得更小的文件大小和更好的性能。您可以传递一个 [SVGO 配置对象](https://svgo.dev/) 来自定义优化：

如果您之前使用了 experimental.svgo，请更新您的配置以改用新的 svgOptimizer 选项。

更多信息，请参阅[实验性 SVG 优化文档](https://docs.astro.build/en/reference/experimental-flags/svg-optimization/)。

实验性日志记录器

现在可以通过自定义日志记录器来控制 Astro 日志记录器。作为实验性功能的一部分，Astro 内置了多种日志记录器，例如 JSON 日志记录器。如果您使用编码代理来处理 Astro，结构化日志将极大地改善您的工作流程。

JSON 日志记录器

您可以通过配置使用 experimental.logger 启用 JSON 日志记录，或通过 CLI 使用 --experimentalJson 标志：

自定义日志记录器

您也可以通过实现正确的接口来创建自己的自定义日志记录器：

有关在项目中启用和使用此功能的更多信息，请参阅[实验性日志记录器文档](https://docs.astro.build/en/reference/experimental-flags/logger/)。

有关此实验性 API 的完整概述和反馈，请参阅[自定义日志记录器 RFC](https://github.com/withastro/roadmap/blob/logger/proposals/0059-custom-logger.md)。

实验性：getFontFileURL()

您现在可以使用从 astro:assets 导出的新 experimental_getFontFileURL() 辅助工具，在预渲染期间加载字体文件数据。这使得在构建时使用 Astro 管理的字体与 [Satori](https://github.com/vercel/satori) 等工具生成 Open Graph 图像成为可能，而以前这需要使用未记录的内部路径进行变通。

更多信息，请参阅[字体 API 文档](https://docs.astro.build/en/guides/fonts/#accessing-font-data-programmatically)。

Astro v7 alpha

Astro 下一个主要版本的开发工作已经开始。首个 Astro 7 alpha 包含两个重大变化：

Vite 8 支持

Astro 7 升级到了 [Vite 8](https://vite.dev/blog/announcing-vite8)，这是驱动 Astro 的构建工具的最新主要版本。对于依赖 Vite 内部机制的 Astro 集成和插件来说，这是一个破坏性变更，但大多数 Astro 用户应该可以在不更改项目代码的情况下进行升级。

稳定的 Rust 编译器

基于 Rust 的 Astro 编译器（之前作为实验性标志 experimental.rustCompiler 提供）现在已成为 Astro 7 中的默认且唯一的编译器。与之前基于 Go 的编译器相比，Rust 编译器提供了显著更快的构建速度。experimental.rustCompiler 标志不再需要，可以从您的配置中移除。

尝试一下

Astro 7 alpha 现已可供早期测试：

我们期待您的反馈！如果您遇到任何问题，请报告。

items.article_text: iPhone 专区 iPhone iPad Mac Vision Watch 搜索 Today 游戏 App 平台 iPhone iPad Mac Vision Watch MyDooors-自然疗愈 · 放松解压 · 收集与装扮 新疆｜手绘场景｜每日礼物｜房间装扮｜安静片刻 仅适用于 iPhone 免费 · App 内购买 · 专为 iPhone 设计。未针对 macOS 验证。 分享 17个评分 5.0 年龄 4+ 岁 类别 健康健美 开发者 Hangzhou Rossy Rossy Technology Co., Ltd 语言 ZH + 1 种语言 大小 178.2 MB iPhone ▶工作累了，生活节奏快，想有个透透气的空间？ 打开My Dooors，打开一扇门，进入自然，聆听大自然的声音，你可以做任何你想做的事情，或者什么都不做。就像一场短暂的旅行，带回一个小礼物，把自然带回家，自由地装扮自己的空间，在自己的专属空间里，慢慢地生活。 ▶在这里你可以： （1）进入手绘的新疆风景里，沉浸在自然白噪声中，体验手绘的治愈感，找回身体的放松节奏 雾气逐渐散去的禾木、冬日初雪的牧场、喀纳斯河边的草地、车上沿途看风景……风声、鸟鸣、牛哞、远处村庄里炊火声、脚踩在雪地的声音……丰富立体的自然声音，把你瞬间拉进其中，跳脱当下。每一种声音都经过手绘场景的重新诠释，让你在喧嚣世界里找到自己的安静角落。 （2）每天获得一件自然礼物和它的故事，共100件礼物 玩偶、照片、地毯、植物等，禾木百年老树的照片，比脸还大的烤馕照片，赛里木湖的白番红花，哈萨克毡房小摆件，森林雪景地毯等……每个礼物都有它的故事，娓娓道来 （3）用礼物装扮你的专属空间 你可以把礼物自由地摆进自己的房间，做出只属于你的 cozy 小世界。也可以在房间里专注、休息、阅读，听听音乐，看着窗外发发呆也好。 （4）每天只需要一点点时间，让生活慢下来 在这里，你没有任务、没有压力。 你可以放松、专注、发呆…… 也可以什么都不做。 ▶为什么会有 MyDooors？ 灵感来自我们的新疆旅行： “那天，我们在山里等待雾散开，突然之间，世界安静、辽阔、温柔，我希望把那种感觉放进一扇门里，让更多人能随时回到那里。” ▶付费与订阅说明 MyDooors 提供以下付费方式： * 月度旅程（订阅）：按月使用 * 年度旅程（订阅）：按年使用 * 永久通行证（一次性购买）：一次开通，永久使用 items.article_title_extracted: ‎MyDooors-自然疗愈 · 放松解压 · 收集与装扮 App - App Store
items.description:
▶工作累了，生活节奏快，想有个透透气的空间？
打开My Dooors，打开一扇门，进入自然，聆听大自然的声音，你可以做任何你想做的事情，或者什么都不做。就像一场短暂的旅行，带回一个小礼物，把自然带回家，自由地装扮自己的空间，在自己的专属空间里，慢慢地生活。

▶在这里你可以：
（1）进入手绘的新疆风景里，沉浸在自然白噪声中，体验手绘的治愈感，找回身体的放松节奏
雾气逐渐散去的禾木、冬日初雪的牧场、喀纳斯河边的草地、车上沿途看风景……风声、鸟鸣、牛哞、远处村庄里炊火声、脚踩在雪地的声音……丰富立体的自然声音，把你瞬间拉进其中，跳脱当下。每一种声音都经过手绘场景的重新诠释，让你在喧嚣世界里找到自己的安静角落。

（2）每天获得一件自然礼物和它的故事，共100件礼物
玩偶、照片、地毯、植物等，禾木百年老树的照片，比脸还大的烤馕照片，赛里木湖的白番红花，哈萨克毡房小摆件，森林雪景地毯等……每个礼物都有它的故事，娓娓道来

（3）用礼物装扮你的专属空间
你可以把礼物自由地摆进自己的房间，做出只属于你的 cozy 小世界。也可以在房间里专注、休息、阅读，听听音乐，看着窗外发发呆也好。

（4）每天只需要一点点时间，让生活慢下来
在这里，你没有任务、没有压力。 你可以放松、专注、发呆…… 也可以什么都不做。

▶为什么会有 MyDooors？
灵感来自我们的新疆旅行： “那天，我们在山里等待雾散开，突然之间，世界安静、辽阔、温柔，我希望把那种感觉放进一扇门里，让更多人能随时回到那里。”

▶付费与订阅说明
MyDooors 提供以下付费方式：
* 月度旅程（订阅）：按月使用
* 年度旅程（订阅）：按年使用
* 永久通行证（一次性购买）：一次开通，永久使用
部分功能需要订阅 MyDooors Pro 才可使用，包括完整的自然场景与小屋体验。

▶订阅自动续费规则：
* 购买后，费用将从您的 Apple ID 账户中扣除
* 订阅将在当前周期结束前 24 小时内自动续费，并从账户扣除续费费用
* 如需取消订阅，请在当前周期结束前至少 24 小时前，在 App Store 账户设置中关闭自动续费
* 取消后，当前周期结束前仍可继续使用已订阅内容
* 若提供免费试用，试用期结束后将自动转为付费订阅并从账户扣费（如未在试用期结束前取消）

▶解锁内容
开通 MyDooors Pro 后，您将获得完整体验：
* 解锁全部自然场景（山间、森林、湖谷等）
* 解锁完整礼物与故事内容（持续收集与更新）
* 解锁小屋布置功能，自由构建属于自己的空间

▶隐私政策： 
https://denim-trollius-d2e.notion.site/Privacy-Policy-2e08c5dc9fa880ea9516e77ef7bfd757
▶用户协议： 
https://denim-trollius-d2e.notion.site/Terms-of-Use-2e08c5dc9fa880ee8bb5f1ac9a348865

items.article_title_extracted: Casa – your home, on autopilot
items.description:
Casa offers a comprehensive membership service designed to simplify home management with a focus on convenience and expert assistance. Key features include:

1. **Home Understanding**: Casa learns about every aspect of your home, documenting items, data points, and serial numbers.
2. **Expert Services**: Members have access to experienced handymen and a dedicated concierge to handle home projects and tasks efficiently, including maintenance and grocery pickups.
3. **Daily Chores Assistance**: The service covers a wide range of tasks from minor fixes (like tightening towel racks) to scheduling pest control and lawn care.
4. **Membership Benefits**: Each month, members receive 1.5 hours of handyman service, with rollover hours, along with unlimited concierge support.
5. **Automated Maintenance**: Users can subscribe to tailored care plans that automate scheduling and management to prevent costly repairs.
6. **Home Inventory**: Members benefit from a detailed inventory of their home’s assets, which aids in maintenance and upgrades.
7. **Pricing**: Membership costs $199/month, with no long-term commitments or hidden fees. 

The service is available in the San Francisco Bay Area and Los Angeles.
items.description_zh:
Casa 提供全面的会员服务，旨在简化家庭管理，注重便捷性与专家协助。核心功能包括：

1. **房屋档案建立**：Casa 会全面了解您的住宅信息，记录物品、数据点及序列号。
2. **专家服务**：会员可联系经验丰富的维修工及专属管家，高效处理家居项目与日常事务，包括维护保养和生鲜代购。
3. **日常杂务协助**：服务覆盖从拧紧毛巾架等小修小补，到安排虫害防治、草坪养护等各类任务。
4. **会员权益**：每月享有1.5小时维修工服务（可累积），另附无限次管家支持。
5. **自动化维护**：可订阅定制化养护方案，自动安排与管理日程，避免高额维修费用。
6. **家居资产清单**：会员可获取详细的住宅资产清单，助力维护与升级规划。
7. **定价方案**：会员费为199美元/月，无长期合约或隐藏费用。

该服务目前覆盖旧金山湾区及洛杉矶地区。

items.article_text:
April 24, 2026
Mediabunny now supports HLS!
Vanilagy
Mediabunny v1.42.0 adds support for HTTP Live Streaming (HLS). This has been in the making for the last ~4 months and is, by far, the biggest addition to Mediabunny since its original release. Like the rest of Mediabunny, it has been implemented from scratch with zero dependencies and is tiny (adds about ~30 kB of additional bundle size).
If you wanna jump right into detailed guides for reading and writing HLS, check out [Reading HLS](https://mediabunny.dev/guide/reading-hls) and [Writing HLS](https://mediabunny.dev/guide/writing-hls). For the full release notes, see [v1.42.0](https://github.com/Vanilagy/mediabunny/releases/tag/v1.42.0).
What is HTTP Live Streaming (HLS)? [](https://mediabunny.dev#what-is-http-live-streaming-hls)
For those unfamiliar, HLS is a protocol used to stream media over the internet using HTTP. At its core, it chunks a long piece of media (like a TV episode) into many short segments, each of which is individually addressable via HTTP, and then provides an index called a "playlist" to the user telling them about the available segments. The client then requests whichever segments it needs.
HLS also enables multiple variants and renditions of the same contents. Typically, this means the same video/audio content encoded with multiple bitrates, resolutions and codecs, and multiple audio tracks, one for each language.
Supported features [](https://mediabunny.dev#supported-features)
One of the reasons that HLS has been in the works for so long is because I've been extremely thorough with the supported feature set.
Mediabunny supports:
- HLS reading and writing
- both VOD and live content
- both unencrypted and encrypted content (including DRM-protected content)
- an arbitrary number of video and audio tracks
- any configuration of variant streams and media renditions
- all segment formats (MPEG-TS, CMAF, fMP4, ADTS, MP3, WAV, ...)
- all codecs (H.264, HEVC, AV1, AAC, MP3, WAVE, AC-3, E-AC-3, ...)
- full lazy loading of track metadata and media, minimizing HTTP requests
- logarithmic seeking performance
- single-file segments via #EXT-X-BYTERANGE and HTTP range requests
- track metadata information (language, name, primary track, autoselect, ...)
- full master playlist configuration for writing
- datetime-stamped media data via #EXT-X-PROGRAM-DATE-TIME
- mid-stream discontinuities
- I-frame only video tracks via #EXT-X-I-FRAME-STREAM-INF
- more, probably
Most of the above features are implemented symmetrically, meaning they are available for both reading and writing operations.
To be fully transparent, these features are not yet supported:
- Subtitles (WebVTT, CEA-608/708, ...)
- ID3v2 metadata extraction
- Writing encrypted segments
- Low-latency HLS
- Built-in analytics, ABR, or CMCD (this is for the user to do)
Difference to existing solutions like hls.js [](https://mediabunny.dev#difference-to-existing-solutions-like-hls-js)
The way Mediabunny enables interaction with HLS playlists is fundamentally different from how existing tools such as [hls.js](https://github.com/video-dev/hls.js) (give them a star!) do it. To put it simply, hls.js is to Mediabunny what a <video>
element is to FFmpeg's C API: one offers a simple, playback-focused developer experience while the other provides fine-grained control over tracks, media samples, decoding, and much more.
Mediabunny is not an HLS player. Mediabunny can be used to build an HLS player, and it can do many things beyond that.
What can it do? [](https://mediabunny.dev#what-can-it-do)
The API surface added by the HLS update is vast and I obviously can't cover it in this announcement. But, here are just some cool things that Mediabunny now enables:
Downloading an HLS playlist as an MP4 [](https://mediabunny.dev#downloading-an-hls-playlist-as-an-mp4)
By using the Conversion API, you can just do this:
import { ... } from 'mediabunny';
const input = new Input({
source: new UrlSource('https://example.com/playlist.m3u8'
items.article_text_zh:
2026年4月24日  
Mediabunny 现已支持 HLS！  
Vanilagy  

Mediabunny v1.42.0 新增了对 HTTP Live Streaming (HLS) 的支持。这一功能在过去约 4 个月内持续开发，是 Mediabunny 自最初发布以来规模最大的一次更新。与 Mediabunny 的其他部分一样，该功能完全从零实现，零依赖，且体积小巧（仅增加约 30 kB 的打包体积）。  

如果你想直接查看关于读取和写入 HLS 的详细指南，请参阅[读取 HLS](https://mediabunny.dev/guide/reading-hls) 和[写入 HLS](https://mediabunny.dev/guide/writing-hls)。完整发布说明请见 [v1.42.0](https://github.com/Vanilagy/mediabunny/releases/tag/v1.42.0)。  

什么是 HTTP Live Streaming (HLS)？ [](https://mediabunny.dev#what-is-http-live-streaming-hls)  

对于不熟悉的人来说，HLS 是一种通过 HTTP 在互联网上流式传输媒体的协议。其核心是将一段较长的媒体（如电视剧集）分割成许多短片段，每个片段均可通过 HTTP 单独寻址，然后向用户提供一个称为“播放列表”的索引，告知用户可用的片段信息。客户端随后请求所需的片段。  

HLS 还支持同一内容的多个变体和呈现版本。通常，这意味着同一视频/音频内容以多种比特率、分辨率和编解码器编码，以及多个音频轨道（每种语言一个）。  

支持的功能 [](https://mediabunny.dev#supported-features)  

HLS 功能开发耗时如此之久的原因之一，是我对支持的功能集进行了极其详尽的考量。  

Mediabunny 支持：  
- HLS 读取和写入  
- VOD 和直播内容  
- 未加密和加密内容（包括 DRM 保护的内容）  
- 任意数量的视频和音频轨道  
- 任意配置的变体流和媒体呈现  
- 所有片段格式（MPEG-TS、CMAF、fMP4、ADTS、MP3、WAV 等）  
- 所有编解码器（H.264、HEVC、AV1、AAC、MP3、WAVE、AC-3、E-AC-3 等）  
- 轨道元数据和媒体的完全懒加载，最大限度减少 HTTP 请求  
- 对数级查找性能  
- 通过 #EXT-X-BYTERANGE 和 HTTP 范围请求实现的单文件片段  
- 轨道元数据信息（语言、名称、主轨道、自动选择等）  
- 写入时的完整主播放列表配置  
- 通过 #EXT-X-PROGRAM-DATE-TIME 实现的时间戳媒体数据  
- 流中不连续性  
- 通过 #EXT-X-I-FRAME-STREAM-INF 实现的仅 I 帧视频轨道  
- 可能还有更多  

上述大部分功能均对称实现，即读取和写入操作均可使用。  

坦率地说，以下功能尚未支持：  
- 字幕（WebVTT、CEA-608/708 等）  
- ID3v2 元数据提取  
- 写入加密片段  
- 低延迟 HLS  
- 内置分析、ABR 或 CMCD（这些由用户自行实现）  

与 hls.js 等现有解决方案的区别 [](https://mediabunny.dev#difference-to-existing-solutions-like-hls-js)  

Mediabunny 与 HLS 播放列表交互的方式，从根本上不同于 hls.js（[给他们点个星吧！](https://github.com/video-dev/hls.js)）等现有工具。简单来说，hls.js 之于 Mediabunny，就如同 `<video>` 元素之于 FFmpeg 的 C API：前者提供简单、以播放为核心的开发者体验，而后者则提供对轨道、媒体样本、解码等内容的精细控制。  

Mediabunny 不是一个 HLS 播放器。Mediabunny 可用于构建 HLS 播放器，并且还能做许多超越播放的事情。  

它能做什么？ [](https://mediabunny.dev#what-can-it-do)  

HLS 更新所增加的 API 接口非常庞大，显然无法在此公告中一一涵盖。但以下只是 Mediabunny 现在能够实现的一些酷炫功能：  

将 HLS 播放列表下载为 MP4 [](https://mediabunny.dev#downloading-an-hls-playlist-as-an-mp4)  

通过使用转换 API，你可以直接这样做：  

```typescript
import { ... } from 'mediabunny';
const input = new Input({
  source: new UrlSource('https://example.com/playlist.m3u8')
});
```

items.article_text_zh:
# Claude Security

## 从扫描到修复，无缝完成

Claude 扫描你的代码库，验证发现结果，并建议可供审查和批准的补丁。现以公开测试版形式面向 Claude Enterprise 用户提供。

[联系销售](#) [阅读教程](#) [播放视频](#)

## 团队如何使用 Claude Security

Claude 像资深安全研究员一样推理你的代码。它能理解上下文、追踪数据流，并捕捉模式匹配工具遗漏的漏洞。然后它会建议修复方案。

### 并行扫描你的代码

Claude 能理解上下文，跨文件追踪数据流，并识别传统扫描器可能无法检测的复杂多组件漏洞模式。

### 验证发现结果

每个发现结果都会经过对抗性验证。Claude 在呈现结果之前会先挑战自己的判断。更多真实问题被报告，更少误报浪费分析师的时间。

### 审查和修补

Claude 检测问题并提出修复方案。每个发现结果都包含一个推荐的补丁，供团队审查和批准。快速修复漏洞，而不是让它们堆积在待办事项中。

## Claude Security 现以公开测试版形式推出

即日起面向 Enterprise 客户提供。

[了解更多](#)

## 为安全团队打造，由 Anthropic 使用

### 透明度和可见性

查看每个发现结果的详细信息：漏洞是什么、为何重要以及建议的修复方案

### 针对性补丁

保持代码结构和风格的修复建议

### 适配现有工作流

通过 webhook 将发现结果推送到 Slack 或 Jira，导出用于审计，并安排定期扫描

### 完全修复控制

团队始终掌控全局，每个补丁都需要人工审查和批准

## 我们最强大的通用模型

由 Anthropic 用于保护自身代码库的相同模型驱动

[上一页](#) [下一页](#)

## 安全资源

[联系销售](#) [阅读教程](#)

## Claude Security 现以公开测试版形式推出

[博客](#) [评估与缓解](#)

items.article_text:
[ Read Now](https://about.divine.video/blog/divine-launches-in-app-stores/)
500,000 classic Vines with new content from OG Viners including Lele Pons, Jimmy Here, MightyDuck and Jack & Jack
“(With Divine)… they are finally correcting every mistake,” Jack Dorsey
[Divine](http://divine.video/), the new six-second looping video app that reimagines the spirit of early internet human creativity by restoring Vine classics and banning AI slop, today launched in the Apple App Store and on Google Play.
Divine was announced in beta at the end of last year with a vision to put creative power back in human hands, and has since restored access to approximately 500,000 archived Vine videos from nearly 100k OG Vine creators including Lele Pons, Hannah Stocking, Jack and Jack, King Bach and Logan and Jake Paul, while enabling new six-second creations.
While rooted in the cultural legacy of short-form video, Divine is already seeing OG Viners including [Lele Pons](https://lelepons.divine.video/), [JimmyHere](https://jimmyhere.divine.video/), [MightyDuck](https://mightyduck.divine.video/), and [Jack and Jack,](https://jackandjackofficial.divine.video/) reclaim their accounts, curate their classics and create new content.
Lele Pons, whose career took-off on Vine while she was in high school, reached out to Divine’s founder, Rabble, within hours of hearing about the app back in November.
“Many of us came from Vine and it was the beginning of everything. An iconic app. It was such a key moment in my own personal journey, and in internet culture, it makes me so happy to see these early classics brought back to life, and to have the chance to make new ones,” said Pons
JimmyHere said: “Vine to me was the golden age of short form content. No AI. No crazy brand deals. Just a bunch of people with really crappy phone quality trying to entertain. With Divine we’re going back to basics. I always felt the six-second restriction wasn’t as much of a restriction, as an art form.”
MightyDuck said: “I would be nothing without Vine… I am so excited about how my videos are about to resurface on Divine… and so excited to make new six-second content… it feels like I’m going home to where I belong!”
Jack Dorsey has provided grant funding for Divine through [And Other Stuff,](http://andotherstuff.org/) the collective he funded to create and steward freedom tech through open tools, protocols, and applications that strengthen human liberty.
Accordingly, Divine has been built on the following principles:
- Creator ownership: creators retain control over their content and hence, their livelihoods
- Human-first content: a place for authentic, non-AI-generated media
- Deliberate design: focused on creativity and constraint over engagement for an ad algorithm
- Open and transparent: built on an open protocol, Nostr, with open source code
“By bringing back Vine on a decentralized network, they are finally correcting every mistake,” said Jack Dorsey.
“It is no secret that we didn’t find a business model for Vine. A founding principle for Divine is that creators will always be in full control of their content and followers, enabling them to create and grow their own revenue streams. I anticipate that Divine will provide a host of tools and services to support the growth of the creator economy,” said Dorsey.
As Divine emerges from beta, what started as an experiment to restore a formative era of internet culture is evolving into a place for creative expression that gives creators full agency over their content and their followers.
“Divine began as a personal project to reconnect with a time when the internet felt creative, open, and unquestionably human,” said Rabble, founder of Divine, and an original member of the team that created Twitter. “The overwhelming response we got to our initial announcement has turned my side project into more of a movement. The app launch is less about nostalgia, and more an antidote to what social media has become.
“Last year I crafted a [new soci
items.article_text_zh:
[立即阅读](https://about.divine.video/blog/divine-launches-in-app-stores/)  
50万条经典Vine视频，以及来自Lele Pons、Jimmy Here、MightyDuck和Jack & Jack等OG Vine创作者的原创内容  
“（有了Divine）……他们终于纠正了每一个错误。”——Jack Dorsey  

[Divine](http://divine.video/)，这款全新的六秒循环视频应用，通过恢复Vine经典内容并禁止AI垃圾内容，重新诠释了早期互联网人类创造力的精神，今日在苹果App Store和Google Play正式上线。  

Divine于去年年底以测试版形式发布，愿景是将创作力量新交还人类手中。此后，它已恢复约50万条存档Vine视频的访问权限，这些视频来自近10万名OG Vine创作者，包括Lele Pons、Hannah Stocking、Jack and Jack、King Bach、Logan和Jake Paul，同时支持用户创作新的六秒视频。  

尽管根植于短视频的文化遗产，Divine已吸引包括[Lele Pons](https://lelepons.divine.video/)、[JimmyHere](https://jimmyhere.divine.video/)、[MightyDuck](https://mightyduck.divine.video/)和[Jack and Jack](https://jackandjackofficial.divine.video/)在内的OG Vine创作者重新认领账号、整理经典作品并创作新内容。  

Lele Pons的职业生涯始于高中时期的Vine。去年11月，她在听说Divine后的几小时内就联系了创始人Rabble。  

“我们很多人都是从Vine起步的，那是一切的起点。一款标志性的应用。它在我个人旅程和互联网文化中都是如此关键的时刻。看到这些早期经典作品重获新生，并有机会创作新内容，我无比开心。”Pons表示。  

JimmyHere说：“对我来说，Vine是短视频内容的黄金时代。没有AI，没有疯狂的品牌合作。只有一群用着糟糕手机画质的人努力逗乐大家。有了Divine，我们回归了本质。我一直觉得六秒限制与其说是限制，不如说是一种艺术形式。”  

MightyDuck说：“没有Vine就没有今天的我……我的视频即将在Divine上重现，我无比兴奋……也无比期待创作新的六秒内容……感觉就像回到了属于我的家！”  

Jack Dorsey通过[And Other Stuff](http://andotherstuff.org/)为Divine提供了资助。该组织由他出资创建，旨在通过开放工具、协议和应用来创造并维护自由技术，以增强人类自由。  

相应地，Divine基于以下原则构建：  
- 创作者所有权：创作者保留对其内容的控制权，从而掌控自身生计  
- 人类优先的内容：一个真实、非AI生成媒体的空间  
- 深思熟虑的设计：专注于创造力和约束，而非为广告算法追求用户参与度  
- 开放透明：基于开放协议Nostr构建，代码开源  

“通过去中心化网络带回Vine，他们终于纠正了每一个错误。”Jack Dorsey表示。  

“众所周知，我们当年没为Vine找到商业模式。Divine的一个基本原则是，创作者始终完全掌控自己的内容和粉丝，从而能够创造并发展自己的收入来源。我预计Divine将提供一系列工具和服务，支持创作者经济的发展。”Dorsey说。  

随着Divine走出测试阶段，这个最初旨在恢复互联网文化一个形成时期的小实验，正演变为一个让创作者对其内容和粉丝拥有完全自主权的创意表达空间。  

“Divine最初只是我个人项目，为了重新连接那个互联网充满创意、开放且毫无疑问属于人类的时代。”Divine创始人、Twitter创始团队成员之一Rabble说。“我们最初公告获得的巨大反响，把我的副业项目变成了一场运动。这次应用上线与其说是怀旧，不如说是对社交媒体现状的一剂解药。  

“去年我制定了一套新的社会

**About this page**  
  
Our systems have detected unusual traffic from your computer network. This page checks to see if it's really you sending the requests, and not a robot. [Why did this happen?](#)  
  
This page appears when Google automatically detects requests coming from your computer network which appear to be in violation of the [Terms of Service](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hTdSU7q5WCo//www.google.com/policies/terms/). The block will expire shortly after those requests stop. In the meantime, solving the above CAPTCHA will let you continue to use our services.  
  
This traffic may have been sent by malicious software, a browser plug-in, or a script that sends automated requests. If you share your network connection, ask your administrator for help — a different computer using the same IP address may be responsible. [Learn more](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hTdSU7q5WCo//support.google.com/websearch/answer/86640)  
  
Sometimes you may be asked to solve the CAPTCHA if you are using advanced terms that robots are known to use, or sending requests very quickly.

  
IP address: 104.28.161.197  
Time: 2026-05-17T14:16:20Z  
URL: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hTdSU7q5WCo

items.article_text_zh:
## 面向开发者与智能体的可编程金融

通过完整的银行 API、终端原生 CLI 和 AI 就绪的 MCP 服务器，自动化您的财务运营。

[获取 API 令牌] [开设账户]

---

### 使用 Mercury CLI 在终端中管理财务

[安装 CLI]

- 从终端、脚本或 CI 流水线中执行 Mercury 操作
- 输出结果人类可读，同时结构化设计便于管道传输、脚本处理和 LLM 消费
- 支持的操作包括：付款、交易分类、创建发票、上传收据

---

### 将您的 AI 智能体接入经过验证的财务数据

[连接 Mercury MCP]

- 让您的智能体拥有与您相同的财务上下文——无需编写代码
- 用自然语言查询余额、交易历史和账户数据
- 让 AI 工作流直接访问实时账户数据——无需脆弱的爬虫或 CSV 导出
- 只读访问确保 AI 可以分析数据，但无法转移资金

---

### 为您的日常工作流而生，无论您在哪里工作

Mercury 可作为原生 MCP 服务器使用，也可通过您的团队已在使用的工作工具访问，包括：

---

### 用于程序化银行操作的 API

[查看 API 文档]

- 通过单一 REST API 拉取余额、列出交易并发起 ACH 转账
- 在沙盒中安全测试，然后只需切换一个 URL 即可进入生产环境
- 每个账户每月包含 100 笔免费 ACH 转账

---

### 编程管理您的应收账款工作流

[查看 AR 文档]

- 基于 CRM、计费或使用数据生成并发送发票
- 监控发票状态，确保您始终按时收款
- 读取发票数据并跟踪您的收入
- Mercury Plus 计划每月最多创建 500 张发票，Pro 计划无限制

---

### 在每一层维护安全与控制

- 颁发具有细粒度权限的作用域 API 令牌
- 强制执行 IP 白名单，并区分读取与写入操作
- 基于 SOC 2 Type II 合规基础设施构建
- 银行服务由 FDIC 保险的合作银行提供，传输和存储数据均经过加密

---

### 您可以使用 Mercury 自动化什么

**夜间对账至 QuickBooks**  
将昨天的交易直接推送至 QuickBooks。

items.article_text: Skip to content Search for: Search Menu Bear.app Newsletter More Tips Updates News Interviews Support Search Settings Night Mode Text Size A Bear: Flexible notes, beautiful writing on Mac, iPad, iPhone, and Apple Watch Bear 2.8: BearCLI, Claude Connector, and MCP server Posted on Published April 29, 2026 April 29, 2026 by Zowie Huang AI has become a genuinely useful part of how a lot of people work, and we’ve spent a good while thinking about what that means for Bear. We didn’t want to bolt something on, hand your notes to a third party. Instead, we built something that gets out of the way and lets you decide what connects to Bear and how. Today we’re introducing BearCLI with Claude Connector and MCP server to open a whole new world for developers, power users, and anyone who wants to connect Bear to the tools they already use. Bear CLI BearCLI is a command-line tool bundled directly inside Bear. It lets you read, search, create, and edit notes from outside the app from a terminal, a script, or any tool that can run a shell command. Run bearcli -h to see everything it can do. For full installation instructions, see the support page . Claude Connector For Claude users, this is the most direct way to put your notes to work. Install it from Bear’s top menu Help → Advanced → Install Claude Connector , and Bear handles the rest. After that, you can open Claude and have a real conversation with your notes: ask questions, make changes, or create new ones, all in natural language. It’s built on BearCLI under the hood, so everything that works in the CLI works here too. Once you set up, the kinds of things you can ask become surprisingly useful, and occasionally a little philosophical: “Find all notes tagged #todo that haven’t been edited in over 30 days” “List all notes I created this week, sorted by last modified” “Find all empty notes and move them to the trash” “Based on everything in my notes, what kind of person do you think I am?” That last one is our favorite. MCP Server The MCP Server is for tools that speak the MCP protocol like Claude Code or certain IDEs. If you’re building workflows or working in an environment that supports MCP, bearcli mcp-server gives those tools direct access to your Bear notes with no extra setup. You can read here for more information about MCP server and how to use it. Your notes, your tools When we chose Markdown as Bear’s foundation, the reason wasn’t just technical. It was because an open format means your notes aren’t locked to any single app or use case. Your notes stay yours, whatever happens. We think about the CLI the same way. It’s a general-purpose tool that doesn’t know or care what’s on the other end. Nothing connects to your notes unless you set it up, and no notes are shared with any tool until you choose to. This just gives you more ways to use them. Try it now! Update Bear now on macOS to try it out. Kind reminder: It’s always a good idea to make a backup before you start anything adventurous. For the full setup guide and command reference, see the support page . Reach out to us on our Community Forum , Reddit , X (Twitter) , Bluesky , or directly at bear@shinyfrog.net to tell us what you build with BearCLI, we’d love to know! Happy writing! 🐻❤️📝 Mac updates Updates « Bear Beyond Text: Working with Images, PDFs, and more Bear at Apple Developer Academy Naples » Shiny Frog, ltd. Community Forum X(Twitter) Reddit RSS Loading Comments... Write a Comment... Email (Required) Name (Required) Website
items.article_text_zh:
跳到内容 搜索： 搜索菜单 Bear.app 新闻简报 更多技巧 更新 新闻 支持 搜索 设置 夜间模式 文字大小 A Bear：在 Mac、iPad、iPhone 和 Apple Watch 上灵活记笔记、优雅写作

Bear 2.8：BearCLI、Claude 连接器与 MCP 服务器

发表于 2026年4月29日 2026年4月29日 作者：Zowie Huang

AI 已经成为许多人工作中真正实用的一部分，我们花了很长时间思考这对 Bear 意味着什么。我们不想简单粗暴地添加一个功能，把你的笔记交给第三方。相反，我们构建了一些不碍事的东西，让你自己决定如何连接 Bear 以及以何种方式连接。今天，我们推出 BearCLI，附带 Claude 连接器和 MCP 服务器，为开发者、高级用户以及任何希望将 Bear 与现有工具连接的人打开一个全新的世界。

Bear CLI

BearCLI 是一个直接内置于 Bear 中的命令行工具。它允许你从应用外部——通过终端、脚本或任何能运行 shell 命令的工具——读取、搜索、创建和编辑笔记。运行 `bearcli -h` 可以查看它的所有功能。完整的安装说明，请参阅支持页面。

Claude 连接器

对于 Claude 用户来说，这是将你的笔记投入工作的最直接方式。从 Bear 的顶部菜单 `帮助 → 高级 → 安装 Claude 连接器` 进行安装，剩下的交给 Bear 处理。之后，你可以打开 Claude 并与你的笔记进行真正的对话：用自然语言提问、修改或创建新笔记。它底层基于 BearCLI 构建，因此 CLI 中能用的功能这里都能用。

设置完成后，你可以问的问题会变得出奇地有用，偶尔还会带点哲学意味：
- “查找所有标记为 #todo 且超过 30 天未编辑的笔记”
- “列出我这周创建的所有笔记，按最后修改时间排序”
- “查找所有空笔记并将其移至废纸篓”
- “根据我笔记里的所有内容，你觉得我是个什么样的人？”

最后一个是我们最喜欢的。

MCP 服务器

MCP 服务器适用于那些支持 MCP 协议的工具，比如 Claude Code 或某些 IDE。如果你正在构建工作流，或者在支持 MCP 的环境中工作，`bearcli mcp-server` 能让这些工具直接访问你的 Bear 笔记，无需额外设置。你可以在这里阅读更多关于 MCP 服务器及其使用方法的信息。

你的笔记，你的工具

当我们选择 Markdown 作为 Bear 的基础时，原因不仅仅是技术层面的。更是因为开放格式意味着你的笔记不会被锁定在任何一个应用或使用场景中。无论发生什么，你的笔记始终属于你。我们对 CLI 的看法也是如此。它是一个通用工具，不关心另一端是什么。除非你主动设置，否则没有任何东西能连接你的笔记；除非你选择，否则没有任何笔记会与任何工具共享。这仅仅为你提供了更多使用它们的方式。

立即尝试！

现在更新 macOS 上的 Bear 即可体验。温馨提醒：在进行任何冒险操作之前，最好先做个备份。完整的设置指南和命令参考，请参阅支持页面。

在我们的社区论坛、Reddit、X (Twitter)、Bluesky 上联系我们，或直接发送邮件至 bear@shinyfrog.net，告诉我们你用 BearCLI 构建了什么——我们很想知道！

写作愉快！🐻❤️📝

items.article_title_extracted: Dynamic Carousel
items.description: The content discusses carousel controls, focusing on the duration (600ms) and various easing functions for slide motion, including easeInQuad, easeOutQuad, easeInOutQuad, and others. It explains that the easing affects only the motion of slides while video frames progress linearly. Additionally, the 'Drag Freeze' feature enables users to pause the video at 50% during dragging and resumes playback of the last 50% upon release.
items.description_zh: 内容讨论了轮播控制，重点介绍了滑动动画的持续时间（600毫秒）及多种缓动函数，包括easeInQuad、easeOutQuad、easeInOutQuad等。文中说明缓动仅影响滑块的移动，而视频帧仍保持线性播放。此外，“拖拽冻结”功能允许用户在拖拽过程中将视频暂停在50%处，释放后则继续播放剩余50%的内容。
items.title: Dynamic Carousel
items.title_zh: 动态轮播

items.article_text:
Whatever AI platform you’re building new sites and apps with these days, getting a database thrown into the mix is a given. The agent will do the scaffolding and create the schema and code for you, which goes a pretty long way.
When we launched Netlify DB, the beta of Netlify Database, about a year ago (decades in AI-years), we had a more modest goal: we provisioned a database for you to use however you wanted, with or without AI. That was a nice shortcut to provisioning a database, but it quickly got behind today’s standards.
So when it came time to plan beyond the beta, the typical path would have been to tighten the integration with our AI features, apply some tweaks and fixes based on feedback, and remove the beta sticker. But as these agents evolved, simply provisioning a database wasn’t enough to keep them from breaking things.
Instead, we took a different approach.
We learned a lesson which prompted us to more deeply rethink our approach and go beyond a few fixes and tweaks. We realized that for a database solution to be truly integrated, it can’t just be another layer glued on top. It must offer the exact same degree of safe, isolated experimentation that code builders have enjoyed for years.
Today, Netlify becomes the only platform that natively pairs Deploy Previews with completely isolated database branches.
Safe experimentation is key
If your integrated database only has a single environment — “production” — it will break. That’s pretty much a given if you work in a team. But it turns out to be very common even for a solo developer. While building, you or an AI agent will make changes that break the live site. Some other changes you may end up wanting to revert, but rolling back database changes is often a special kind of nightmare.
In some other tools, there’s a built-in concept of a development database vs. a production one. That’s better, but still leaves a lot to be desired:
- How do you manage iterating on multiple features at the same time? Some you want and some you end up shelving.
- What happens if there’s a team involved? How do you untangle sticky conflicts?
Development teams have struggled with all of this as well. Many teams still have a few shared environments (often known as “staging” or “QA”) that are in a continually semi-broken state. Every working day, someone pushes their half-baked work to the same place, then semi-randomly adds or modifies data to test it. The pain is real.
Modern databases do offer a solution: branching. But branching has long been a pro-level feature that one has to know exactly how to set up and use. It’s a raw feature, rather than a workflow.
We’re all expected to be 10x builders today (no stress!). In practical terms, that means trying out more things, building and experimenting more, parallelizing more. Tooling to democratize that is essential. To provide you with a real solution, we went back to first principles. We can’t just provision a database for you and set our AI agent loose. Rather, we have to provide a workflow which bakes in pro-level best practices by default, for everyone.
We’re not trying to re-invent industry standards. We’re sticking to Postgres. All the needed tooling already exists out there. Our job has been to bring it all together.
An opinionated workflow
Here’s the workflow, in concrete terms:
- Whenever you start an agent run with Netlify’s Agent Runners and select the agent of your choice (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini), the agent will first decide whether a database is needed. If so, it provisions one, similar to what any comparable platform would do.
- On Netlify, however, each agent run also automatically gets its own database branch. The new branch is based on the latest production data, but all schema or content changes are contained within this branch.
- As the agent runs and generates new code, it also updates the data schema as needed by generating migration files. These include SQL commands to alter the database structure to its desired form
items.article_text_zh:
无论你当前使用哪个AI平台构建新网站和应用，集成数据库都是必然需求。AI智能体会自动完成脚手架搭建、创建数据库模式和代码，这已经能大幅提升效率。

大约一年前（按AI发展速度相当于数十年），我们推出Netlify DB（Netlify Database的测试版）时，目标相对保守：为用户预配置一个可自由使用的数据库，无论是否借助AI。这在当时是快速获取数据库的捷径，但很快就落后于当今的标准。

因此在规划测试版后续发展时，常规路径本应是：深化与AI功能的集成、根据反馈进行优化修复、移除测试版标签。但随着这些智能体的进化，单纯预配置数据库已不足以防止系统崩溃。

我们选择了不同的道路。

这次教训促使我们彻底反思策略，而非仅做修补。我们意识到：真正集成的数据库解决方案，不能只是简单叠加的附加层。它必须提供与代码构建工具同等程度的安全隔离实验环境。

如今，Netlify成为唯一原生支持"部署预览"与完全隔离数据库分支的云平台。

安全实验是关键

如果集成数据库只有"生产环境"单一环境，必然会出现问题——这在团队协作中几乎是铁律。但事实证明，即便是独立开发者也会频繁遭遇此困境。在开发过程中，你或AI智能体的修改可能破坏线上站点。有些改动需要回滚，但数据库回滚往往是噩梦般的存在。

某些工具内置了开发数据库与生产数据库的区分概念。这虽有改进，但仍存在明显不足：
- 如何同时管理多个功能的迭代？有些功能需要保留，有些则要搁置。
- 团队协作时如何处理棘手的冲突？

开发团队长期受困于此。许多团队仍在使用几个共享环境（通常称为"预发布"或"质量测试"），这些环境长期处于半崩溃状态。每个工作日都有人将未完成的工作推送到同一环境，然后半随机地添加或修改数据来测试。这种痛苦真实存在。

现代数据库确实提供了解决方案：分支。但分支长期以来都是专业级功能，需要精确掌握配置和使用方法。它更像原始功能而非工作流程。

如今我们都被期望成为10倍效率的开发者（压力山大！）。这意味着要尝试更多可能性、加速构建实验、实现并行开发。实现这一目标的关键在于工具民主化。为了提供真正的解决方案，我们回归本源：不能仅预配置数据库就放任AI智能体自由行动。必须构建一个默认内置专业级最佳实践的工作流程，惠及所有用户。

我们无意重新发明行业标准。坚持使用PostgreSQL，所有必要工具都已存在。我们的职责是整合这些资源。

规范化工作流程

具体工作流程如下：
- 当你通过Netlify的Agent Runners启动智能体运行，并选择所需智能体（Claude Code、Codex、Gemini）时，智能体会首先判断是否需要数据库。若需要，则自动预配置数据库——这与同类平台的操作一致。
- 但在Netlify上，每次智能体运行都会自动获得独立的数据库分支。新分支基于最新生产数据创建，所有模式或内容变更都限定在该分支内。
- 当智能体运行并生成新代码时，会通过生成迁移文件同步更新数据模式。这些文件包含用于将数据库结构调整至目标形态的SQL命令。
items.article_title_extracted: Netlify Database is now available: from provisioning to integrated Postgres
items.description: Netlify Database is a fully managed Postgres database built into the Netlify platform. It brings professional branching and migration workflows into every project, so builders can move fast without things constantly breaking.

items.title: Netlify Database is now available: from provisioning to integrated Postgres

Just describe what you want to make, and Lovable builds it — design, logic, backend, and all. You don't need to know how to code. You don't need a technical co-founder. You don't need to wait months for a developer. If you can describe what you want, you can build it today. Millions of people are already using Lovable to create things they never thought they could. Founders have launched revenue-generating businesses. Marketing teams have shipped websites in an afternoon instead of waiting six weeks. Here's how it works: open Lovable, describe your idea in plain language — or drop in a screenshot — and watch it come to life. **Lovable：用 AI 构建应用**  
快速构建网站与工具  
免费 · 含内购 · 专为 iPad 设计 · 未验证 macOS 兼容性  
分享 123 个评分 4.6  
年龄评级：16 岁以上  
类别：生产力  
开发者：Lovable Labs Incorporated  
语言：英文  
大小：41.7 MB  
iPhone、iPad

Lovable 能将你的想法变成真正可用的应用和网站。只需描述你想制作的内容，Lovable 就会为你构建——包括设计、逻辑、后端等一切。你无需懂编程，无需技术联合创始人，也无需等待数月寻找开发者。只要你能描述需求，今天就能动手实现。

已有数百万人使用 Lovable 创建了他们从未想过能完成的作品。创始人用它推出了创收业务；营销团队用一下午就上线了网站，而不是等上六周；产品经理带着可运行的原型去开会，而不是幻灯片。

运作方式如下：打开 Lovable，用日常语言描述你的想法——或直接拖入一张截图——然后看着它变成现实。Lovable 会生成一个完整、可运行的应用，包含真实代码、精美设计，且一切功能已连接好。接着，通过对话进行迭代：调整布局、添加功能、接入支付系统，不断打磨直到完美。准备就绪后，一键部署。

人们在 Lovable 上构建的内容包括：
- Web 应用和移动应用
- 网站和落地页
- 内部工具和仪表盘
- SaaS 产品和市场平台
- 团队定制工具

它与你已有的工具无缝连接——Stripe 用于支付、GitHub 用于代码、Notion 用于文档、Shopify 用于电商、Slack 用于通知，以及数十种其他服务。身份验证、数据库和托管从一开始就包含在内。无需设置、无需配置、无需操心基础设施。

Lovable 从第一天起就是为有想法的人而建，而不仅仅是工程师。这就是它与其他工具不同的原因——从第一刻起就简单易用，却能产出专业品质的成果，让你自豪地分享。已有超过 2500 万个项目在该平台上构建。

免费开始构建。  
条款与政策：https://lovable.dev/terms

更多  
评分与评论

items.description:
Build real products with AI in minutes.
Lovable is an AI software builder that turns your idea into working websites or web apps. No code required.

Create tools and websites fast using natural language. Whether you’re a founder, vibe coder or developer, Lovable enables you to ship apps quickly without coding knowledge.

With Lovable you can:
• Build software with AI
• Turn ideas into real products
• Create web apps
• Prototype ideas fast
• Build without coding or with full code control
• Develop tools to improve your teams productivity
• Vibe coding websites

Perfect for:
• Startup founders
• Hackathons & side projects
• Beginners learning app development
• Developers who want to ship faster
• Teams replacing SaaS tools or building their own
• Vibe coders

Why Lovable?
• AI-powered software builder
• No-code and low-code workflows
• Fast prototyping and instant previews
• Cloud-based, private, and secure
• Developer without needing engineers

Whether you’re experimenting with an idea or building your next startup, Lovable helps you move from concept to app faster. If you’ve used tools like Claude, ChatGPT, Perplexity or Gemini for coding, Lovable helps you go one step further — from prompts to complete, working applications. Describe what you want to build, and Lovable generates the structure, logic, and UI automatically.

Terms & policies: https://lovable.dev/terms

Lovable is an independent product and is not affiliated with ChatGPT, Claude, Replit, Cursor, Bolt, v0, Base44, Wix or Perplexity.

items.article_text:
Creative professionals look to technology to expand what's possible in their work. Claude can't replace taste or imagination, but it can open up new ways of working—faster and more ambitious ideation, a more expansive skill set, and the ability for creatives to take on larger-scale projects. AI can also help shoulder the parts of the creative process that eat up time by handling repetitive tasks and eliminating manual toil. Key to both these goals is integrating Claude into the tools the creative industry already knows and trusts.
Today, we’re releasing a set of connectors—tools that let Claude work alongside the software creative professionals rely on, so creatives can extend their reach.
Connecting Claude to creative tools
Connectors allow Claude to access other platforms and tools directly. We are adding several new connectors that are designed to make it easier to use Claude for creative work:
[Ableton](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.b1e91954-cb9c-4711-8653-65844b570815/directory/connectors/ant.dir.gh.ableton.ableton-knowledge)grounds Claude’s answers in official product documentation for Live and Push.[Adobe for creativity](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.b1e91954-cb9c-4711-8653-65844b570815/directory/connectors/adobe-creativity)enables users to bring images, videos, and designs to life, drawing from 50+ tools across Creative Cloud apps including Photoshop, Premiere, Express, and more.[Affinity by Canva](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.b1e91954-cb9c-4711-8653-65844b570815/directory/connectors/ant.dir.gh.canva.affinity)automates repetitive production tasks across pro creative workflows - such as batch image adjustments, layer renaming, and file export - and generates custom features directly in the app.[Autodesk Fusion](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.b1e91954-cb9c-4711-8653-65844b570815/directory/connectors/ant.dir.gh.autodesk.fusion-mcp)allows designers and engineers with a Fusion subscription to create and modify 3D models through conversations with Claude.[Blender](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.b1e91954-cb9c-4711-8653-65844b570815/directory/connectors/ant.dir.gh.blender.blender-mcp)offers a natural-language interface to its Python API, allowing users to explore and understand complex setups and making it easier to access Blender’s documentation.[Resolume Arena](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.b1e91954-cb9c-4711-8653-65844b570815/directory/connectors/ant.dir.gh.resolume.resolume-arena)and[Resolume Wire](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.b1e91954-cb9c-4711-8653-65844b570815/directory/connectors/ant.dir.gh.resolume.resolume-wire)let VJs and live visual artists control Arena, Avenue, and Wire in real time through natural language for live performance and AV production.[SketchUp](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.b1e91954-cb9c-4711-8653-65844b570815/directory/connectors/sketchup)turns a conversation with Claude into a starting point for 3D modeling—describe a room, a piece of furniture, or a site concept, then open it in SketchUp to refine.[Splice](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.b1e91954-cb9c-4711-8653-65844b570815/directory/connectors/splice)gives music producers the ability to search its catalog of royalty-free samples from within Claude.
Using Claude for creative work
Here are a few of the ways Claude can be used for creative tasks:
- Learning and mastering creative tools: Claude can act as an on-demand tutor for complex software. You can ask it to explain a modifier stack, walk you through a synthesis technique, or demonstrate an unfamiliar feature, and it will show you how to use it.
- Extending tools with code: Claude Code can write scripts, plugins, and generative systems for the software you already use. You can ask it to build a custom shader, script a procedural animation, or generate parametric models, and it will produce documented code you can reuse and modify.
- Bridging tools in a pipeline: Claude can translate formats, restructure
items.article_text_zh:
创意专业人士依靠技术来拓展其工作的可能性。Claude 无法取代品味或想象力，但它可以开启新的工作方式——更快速、更大胆的构思，更广泛的技能组合，以及让创意人员有能力承接更大规模的项目。AI 还能通过处理重复性任务和消除手动劳作，帮助分担创意过程中耗时费力的部分。实现这两个目标的关键，在于将 Claude 集成到创意行业已经熟悉和信赖的工具中。

今天，我们发布了一套连接器——这些工具能让 Claude 与创意专业人士依赖的软件协同工作，从而帮助创意人员延伸其能力范围。

**将 Claude 连接到创意工具**

连接器允许 Claude 直接访问其他平台和工具。我们新增了多个连接器，旨在让 Claude 更易于用于创意工作：

- **[Ableton](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.b1e91954-cb9c-4711-8653-65844b570815/directory/connectors/ant.dir.gh.ableton.ableton-knowledge)**：将 Claude 的回答基于 Live 和 Push 的官方产品文档。
- **[Adobe for creativity](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.b1e91954-cb9c-4711-8653-65844b570815/directory/connectors/adobe-creativity)**：使用户能够借助 Creative Cloud 应用（包括 Photoshop、Premiere、Express 等）中 50 多种工具，让图像、视频和设计作品栩栩如生。
- **[Affinity by Canva](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.b1e91954-cb9c-4711-8653-65844b570815/directory/connectors/ant.dir.gh.canva.affinity)**：在专业创意工作流程中自动化重复性生产任务——例如批量图像调整、图层重命名和文件导出——并直接在应用中生成自定义功能。
- **[Autodesk Fusion](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.b1e91954-cb9c-4711-8653-65844b570815/directory/connectors/ant.dir.gh.autodesk.fusion-mcp)**：允许拥有 Fusion 订阅的设计师和工程师通过与 Claude 对话来创建和修改 3D 模型。
- **[Blender](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.b1e91954-cb9c-4711-8653-65844b570815/directory/connectors/ant.dir.gh.blender.blender-mcp)**：为其 Python API 提供自然语言界面，使用户能够探索和理解复杂的设置，并更轻松地访问 Blender 的文档。
- **[Resolume Arena](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.b1e91954-cb9c-4711-8653-65844b570815/directory/connectors/ant.dir.gh.resolume.resolume-arena)** 和 **[Resolume Wire](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.b1e91954-cb9c-4711-8653-65844b570815/directory/connectors/ant.dir.gh.resolume.resolume-wire)**：让 VJ 和现场视觉艺术家能够通过自然语言实时控制 Arena、Avenue 和 Wire，用于现场表演和视听制作。
- **[SketchUp](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.b1e91954-cb9c-4711-8653-65844b570815/directory/connectors/sketchup)**：将与 Claude 的对话转化为 3D 建模的起点——描述一个房间、一件家具或一个场地概念，然后在 SketchUp 中打开进行细化。
- **[Splice](https://claude.ai/redirect/website.v1.b1e91954-cb9c-4711-8653-65844b570815/directory/connectors/splice)**：让音乐制作人能够在 Claude 内部搜索其免版税样本库。

**使用 Claude 进行创意工作**

以下是 Claude 可用于创意任务的一些方式：

- **学习和掌握创意工具**：Claude 可以作为复杂软件的按需导师。你可以让它解释修改器堆栈、引导你完成合成技术，或演示一个不熟悉的功能，它会向你展示如何使用。
- **通过代码扩展工具**：Claude Code 可以为你已使用的软件编写脚本、插件和生成式系统。你可以让它构建自定义着色器、编写程序化动画脚本或生成参数化模型，它会生成带有文档的代码，供你重复使用和修改。
- **桥接工作流程中的工具**：Claude 可以转换格式、重构

items.article_text:
[Mitchell Hashimoto](https://mitchellh.com/)
Ghostty Is Leaving GitHub
Writing this makes me irrationally sad, but Ghostty will be leaving GitHub[1](https://mitchellh.com#user-content-fn-3).
I'm GitHub user 1299, joined Feb 2008.
Since then, I've opened GitHub every single day. Every day, multiple times per day, for over 18 years. Over half my life. A handful of exceptions in there (I'd love to see the data), but I can't imagine more than a week per year.
GitHub is the place that has made me the most happy. I always made time for it. When I went through tough breakups? I lost myself in open source... on GitHub. During college at 4 AM when everyone is passed out? Let me get one commit in. During my honeymoon while my wife is still asleep? Yeah, GitHub. It's where I've historically been happiest and wanted to be.
Even the annoying stuff! Some people doom scroll social media. I've been doom scrolling GitHub issues since before that was a word. On vacations I'd have bookmarks of different projects on GitHub I wanted to study. Not just source code, but OSS processes, how other maintainers react to difficult situations. Etc. Believe it or not, I like this.
Some might call this sick, but my hobby and work and passion all align and for most of my life they got to also live in one place on the internet: GitHub.
Did you know I started Vagrant (my first successful open source project) in large part because I hoped it would get me a job at GitHub? It's no secret, I've said this repeatedly, and in my first public talk about Vagrant, when I was a mere 20 years old, I joked "maybe GitHub will hire me if it's good!"
GitHub was my dream job. I didn't ever get to work there (not their fault). But it was the perfect place I wanted to be. The engineers were incredible, the product was incredible, and it was something I lived and breathed every day. I still do and consistently have... for these 18 years. Enough time for an entire human to become an adult, all on GitHub.
Lately, I've been very publicly critical of GitHub. I've been mean about it. I've been angry about it. I've hurt people's feelings. I've been lashing out. Because GitHub is failing me, every single day, and it is personal. It is irrationally personal. I love GitHub more than a person should love a thing, and I'm mad at it. I'm sorry about the hurt feelings to the people working on it.
I've felt this way for a long time, but for the past month I've kept a journal
where I put an "X" next to every date where a GitHub outage has negatively
impacted my ability to work[2](https://mitchellh.com#user-content-fn-2). Almost every day has an X. On the day I am
writing this post, I've been unable to do any PR review for ~2 hours because
there is a GitHub Actions outage[3](https://mitchellh.com#user-content-fn-1). This is no longer a place for serious
work if it just blocks you out for hours per day, every day.
It's not a fun place for me to be anymore. I want to be there but it doesn't want me to be there. I want to get work done and it doesn't want me to get work done. I want to ship software and it doesn't want me to ship software.
I want it to be better, but I also want to code. And I can't code with GitHub anymore. I'm sorry. After 18 years, I've got to go. I'd love to come back one day, but this will have to be predicated on real results and improvements, not words and promises.
I'll share more details about where the Ghostty project will be moving to in the coming months. We have a plan but I'm also very much still in discussions with multiple providers (both commercial and FOSS).
It'll take us time to remove all of our dependencies on GitHub and we have a plan in place to do it as incrementally as possible. We plan on keeping a read-only mirror available on GitHub at the current URL.
My personal projects and other work will remain on GitHub for now. Ghostty is where I, our maintainers, and our open source community are most impacted so that is the focus of this change. We'll see where i
items.article_text_zh:
[Mitchell Hashimoto](https://mitchellh.com/)
Ghostty 正在离开 GitHub
写下这些让我感到一种非理性的悲伤，但 Ghostty 将离开 GitHub[1](https://mitchellh.com#user-content-fn-3)。
我是 GitHub 用户 1299，于 2008 年 2 月加入。
从那时起，我每天都会打开 GitHub。每天多次，持续超过 18 年。这占据了我生命的一半以上。期间有少数例外（我很想看看数据），但我想每年不会超过一周。
GitHub 是让我最快乐的地方。我总是为它腾出时间。当我经历艰难的分手时？我沉浸在开源中……在 GitHub 上。大学凌晨 4 点，当所有人都睡着时？让我提交一次代码。蜜月期间，妻子还在睡觉时？是的，GitHub。它一直是我历史上最快乐、最想去的地方。
甚至那些烦人的事情！有些人沉迷于刷社交媒体。我早在“刷”这个词出现之前，就一直在刷 GitHub 的 issue。度假时，我会收藏 GitHub 上想研究的各种项目。不仅仅是源代码，还有开源流程、其他维护者如何应对困难情况等等。信不信由你，我喜欢这些。
有人可能觉得这很病态，但我的爱好、工作和热情完全一致，在我生命的大部分时间里，它们都存在于互联网上的同一个地方：GitHub。
你知道吗，我启动 Vagrant（我的第一个成功的开源项目）很大程度上是希望它能帮我在 GitHub 找到一份工作？这不是秘密，我反复说过，在我 20 岁时第一次公开谈论 Vagrant 的演讲中，我开玩笑说：“如果它做得好，也许 GitHub 会雇用我！”
GitHub 曾是我的梦想工作。我从未在那里工作过（不是他们的错）。但它是我理想中的完美之地。那里的工程师令人难以置信，产品也令人难以置信，这是我每天赖以生存的东西。18 年来，我依然如此，始终如一。这段时间足以让一个完整的人成长为成年人，全部都在 GitHub 上。
最近，我公开批评了 GitHub。我对此很刻薄。我很愤怒。我伤害了别人的感情。我一直在发泄。因为 GitHub 每天都在让我失望，而这很个人化。这是非理性的个人化。我对 GitHub 的爱超过了人应该对事物的爱，而我对它感到愤怒。对于因此伤害到那些为之工作的人的感情，我很抱歉。
我这种感觉已经持续很久了，但过去一个月我一直在记日记，在每次 GitHub 宕机对我工作能力产生负面影响的日期旁边打上“X”[2](https://mitchellh.com#user-content-fn-2)。几乎每天都有 X。在我写这篇文章的今天，我已经有大约 2 个小时无法进行任何 PR 审查，因为 GitHub Actions 宕机了[3](https://mitchellh.com#user-content-fn-1)。如果它每天、每小时都把你挡在门外，这就不再是一个适合严肃工作的地方了。
它对我来说不再是一个有趣的地方。我想待在那里，但它不想让我待在那里。我想完成工作，但它不想让我完成工作。我想发布软件，但它不想让我发布软件。
我希望它能变得更好，但我也希望写代码。而我再也无法在 GitHub 上写代码了。对不起。18 年后，我必须离开了。我很乐意有一天回来，但这必须基于真正的成果和改进，而不是空话和承诺。
我将在未来几个月分享更多关于 Ghostty 项目将迁移到何处的细节。我们有一个计划，但我仍在与多个提供商（包括商业和 FOSS）进行讨论。
我们需要时间来移除所有对 GitHub 的依赖，我们有一个计划，尽可能逐步地完成。我们计划在当前 URL 的 GitHub 上保留一个只读镜像。
我的个人项目和其他工作暂时会留在 GitHub 上。Ghostty 是我、我们的维护者以及我们的开源社区受影响最大的地方，因此这是这次变更的重点。我们拭目以待。
items.article_title_extracted: Ghostty Is Leaving GitHub – Mitchell Hashimoto
items.description: Mitchell Hashimoto announces the departure of Ghostty from GitHub after 18 years of daily use. He shares deep emotional connections to the platform, where he engaged with open source projects during significant life events. However, recent frustrations due to frequent outages and performance issues have led to discontent. Despite his love for GitHub, he feels unable to work effectively there anymore and intends to migrate Ghostty to new platforms. Hashimoto mentions a plan for gradual transition and keeping a read-only mirror on GitHub. He highlights ongoing discussions with other providers and intends to keep personal projects on GitHub for now.
items.description_zh: Mitchell Hashimoto 宣布，在持续 18 年的日常使用后，Ghostty 将离开 GitHub。他分享了自己与这个平台的深厚情感联系——在这里，他参与了多个开源项目，见证了人生中的重要时刻。然而，近期频繁的宕机和性能问题带来的挫败感，让他感到不满。尽管他依然热爱 GitHub，但已无法在那里高效工作，因此计划将 Ghostty 迁移至新平台。Hashimoto 提到将采取渐进式过渡，并在 GitHub 上保留只读镜像。他强调正在与其他服务商进行讨论，同时个人项目暂时仍会留在 GitHub。
items.title: Ghostty Is Leaving GitHub – Mitchell Hashimoto

卡西欧60周年纪念

客户：卡西欧  
服务：导演、插画与动画  
2D–3D动画与合成：Jose Lorenzo  
赛璐珞动画：Christian Villacañas  
音乐与音效：Ohanzo Music  

广告动画  

为卡西欧计算器60周年制作的动画视频，回顾了六十年间致力于让数学更贴近每个人的精彩历程，呈现了品牌最具标志性的几款机型。故事始于1965年的Casio 001——公司首台桌面计算器。到1970年代，卡西欧已进入家庭、办公室和教室，凭借在数学可视化方面的持续创新，成为标志性品牌和真正的先驱。
